The Manager, Business Solutions will be responsible for leading and managing large, complex, cross-functional enterprise initiatives and technology projects leveraging Agile, Scrum, and project management best practices.

The Manager, Business Solutions is responsible for the successful identification, qualification, design, and execution of innovative technology projects within Ryan practices. This individual, under the guidance of their aligned leader, will function as a critical junction point among clients, external resources, internal technology teams, and executive leadership.

This position requires a self-starting, creative problem solver with strong project management experience, hands-on software engineering expertise, and the ability to turn business ideas into scalable technology solutions. The ideal candidate will have experience building applications, integrating AI capabilities into enterprise workflows, and using large language models, including Claude or similar LLM platforms, to drive automation, insight generation, and decision-making.

Flawless client service and a positive attitude are critical to the success of this position. The ideal candidate will have a strong business process, tax and/or technology background, as well as a proven track record of successfully delivering leading-edge projects, contributing to strategic workstreams, and driving digital transformation initiatives to completion.

People

Duties and Responsibilities, aligned to Ryan’s Key Results:


Serves as a business partner to identify high-value transformative projects for assigned practices.
Leads multiple digital transformation projects successfully as Lead Project Manager.
Partners with cross-functional stakeholders and team members to effectively prioritize, plan, and organize the completion of project milestones and work while maintaining strong relationships with assigned practices.
Demonstrates Ryan’s core values by creating a positive, accountable work environment for a growing, entrepreneurial team.
Communicates timely; concise and engaging communications with stakeholders and leadership throughout the project lifecycle and at all levels of the organizations.
Drives excellence by continuously soliciting feedback from internal clients and stakeholders.
Designs and delivers effective Change Management plans to effectively communicate and educate end users.
May require supervisory responsibility to include but not limited to managing projects, training employees, coaching, assigning work, and making sound business decisions while representing the Firm.



Client


Fosters relationships with internal and external stakeholders to secure commitment and drive project success.
Manages customer relationships to ensure the highest quality of customer service is provided.
Escalates and manages project issues and risks to the appropriate stakeholders with clarity, timeliness, and professionalism.
Tracks progress and provides timely status reporting of milestones, dependencies, risks, and issues to initiative owners, key stakeholders, and leadership.
Oversees all scoping, planning and execution, and managing activities to ensure on-time delivery, within budget and meets or exceeds expectations.



Value


Responsible for the execution of a portfolio of greenfield projects, ensuring expectations for scope, schedule, and budget are met.
Supports and guide the analysis of tax data requirements to include, but not limited to: client information systems in complex projects, while ensuring best practices are applied to minimizing the impact to our clients and their IT organizations.
Develops and provides education and coaching to elevate project management knowledge, skills, tools, and techniques to meet project requirements.
Evaluates business processes proactively to identify and execute improvements leading to quantifiable savings to the Firm.
Develops appropriate training and communication plans/content to project stakeholders.
Performs other duties as assigned.



Education And Experience


Bachelor’s degree or Master’s degree in business management, tax, computer science, information systems, engineering, or a related field preferred.
Minimum of 5 years of hands-on software engineering or application development experience.
Minimum of 5 years of experience in implementation, consulting, technology project management, business solutions, or digital transformation, with a progressive record of successful delivery of complex solutions, systems, or services.
Experience building, deploying, or managing enterprise applications, automation solutions, or AI-enabled business tools is strongly preferred.
Experience using large language models, including Claude or similar LLM platforms, to support business process automation, application development, workflow optimization, knowledge management, analytics, or decision support.



Key Experiences


Proven track record of managing and delivering multiple successful enterprise-grade projects with tight deadlines.
Led and participated in successful business process automation projects.
Ability to determine and deploy best methodology for the scale and requirements of the application.
Exposure to modern enterprise applications, and their interfaces (APIs and GUIs).
Demonstrated experience in the development or deployment of one or more enterprise software applications is an advantage .
Exposure to low-code Business Process Management (BPM) solutions and their interaction with RPA is a strong differentiator.
Exposure to Continuous Integration/Continuous Delivery, Agile Scrum, and DevOps methods.
Knowledge of tax, compliance, or accounting processes and applications is a strong differentiator
Five years or more of implementation, consulting, or technology project management.
Lean or Six Sigma certifications and experience preferred.
Demonstrates leadership engagement and influencing skills.
Manages multiple concurrent projects in a cross-functional organization and excels in a dynamic and collaborative environment supporting high-growth and fast-paced business units.
Ability to coach teams to quickly identify core issues and determine solutions while creating structure around ambiguity.
Strong drive for results and continuous improvement.
Outstanding organizational, communication, and interpersonal skills.
Experience integrating LLMs and AI capabilities into enterprise applications and analytics workflows to enhance automation, insight generation, and decision-making in a scalable and governed manner.
Strong experience with Alteryx, including establishing governance frameworks, promoting best practices, and ensuring scalable, secure platform adoption.



Computer Skills

To perform this job successfully, an individual must be proficient in Microsoft® Word, Excel, Access, PowerPoint, Outlook, and Internet navigation and research. Fluency in at least one programming language, scripting language, or automation platform is highly desired. Experience with one or more database platform is also a strong indicator for success in the role.

Certificates And Licenses


PMP Project Management Professional Certification (preferred)
CSM Certified ScrumMaster (preferred)
PMI-ACP Agile Certified Practitioner (preferred)
Six Sigma Green Belt (preferred)
Valid driver’s license (required)



Work Environment


Standard indoor working environment.
Occasional long periods of sitting while working at computer. 
Position requires regular interaction with employees and vendors both in person and via e-mail and telephone.
Travel requirement approximately 30-50%.

About the job




About the Role




Staritas is seeking a Director of Software Engineering to lead, modernize, and scale its engineering organization. This role is responsible for software delivery, team leadership, platform modernization, DevOps maturity, production reliability, software quality, and responsible AI adoption.




The Director will lead engineering managers, technical leads, and software teams building secure, scalable, business-aligned enterprise applications. This leader will partner with Product, Architecture, Data, Security, Infrastructure, Operations, and business stakeholders to turn strategy into clear roadmaps and measurable outcomes.





 






Key Responsibilities





Lead, coach, and develop high-performing software engineering teams.


Own delivery outcomes, including roadmap execution, quality, reliability, team performance, and engineering effectiveness.


Partner with the CTO and technology leaders on engineering strategy, modernization, AI enablement, SDLC improvements, and delivery operations.


Work with Product and business stakeholders to prioritize roadmaps, balance feature delivery with technical health, and align work to business goals.


Establish engineering standards, delivery metrics, agile practices, quality gates, documentation expectations, and release discipline.


Drive modernization of legacy applications, platforms, development practices, deployment models, and workflows.


Improve delivery through CI/CD, automated testing, secure development, observability, incident management, and continuous improvement.


Reduce technical debt, platform fragmentation, manual processes, recurring defects, and production instability.


Build a culture of accountability, collaboration, ownership, learning, quality, and operational excellence.


Lead hiring, onboarding, performance management, career development, succession planning, and team health initiatives.





AI Enablement





Champion responsible AI adoption across software engineering.


Evaluate and guide use of tools such as Claude, ChatGPT, Gemini, GitHub Copilot, Cursor, and AI-enabled IDEs.


Help teams use AI for code generation, reviews, refactoring, testing, documentation, troubleshooting, research, and workflow optimization.


Develop responsible AI standards covering data protection, acceptable use, human review, auditability, IP protection, cost management, and risk mitigation.


Partner with Architecture, Security, Compliance, Legal, Data, and business stakeholders to ensure AI use aligns with enterprise governance.





Required Qualifications





Bachelor’s or master’s degree in Computer Science, IT, Engineering, or equivalent experience.


12+ years of progressive technology experience.


5+ years of engineering leadership experience.


Proven experience leading software delivery, engineering execution, production reliability, and technical quality.


Experience hiring, coaching, and developing engineering managers, technical leads, senior engineers, and individual contributors.


Strong background in enterprise applications, APIs, integrations, cloud platforms, DevOps, CI/CD, automated testing, and production support.


Knowledge of secure SDLC, agile delivery, observability, release management, databases, cloud-native delivery, cybersecurity, and operational resilience.


Hands-on experience using AI tools to improve engineering productivity, quality, documentation, testing, or troubleshooting.





Preferred Qualifications





Experience modernizing enterprise platforms and reducing technical debt.


Experience with AWS, Azure, Google Cloud, or hybrid cloud environments.


Experience with SaaS, ERP, CRM, workflow, analytics, data platforms, or reporting systems.


Familiarity with SAFe, ITIL, Agile, Scrum, Kanban, or similar frameworks.


Experience implementing engineering operating models, delivery metrics, architecture reviews, reusable patterns, and documentation standards.


Experience evaluating or implementing AI platforms, LLM integrations, AI agents, RAG, MCP servers, or AI governance practices.





Success Measures





Engineering teams deliver faster, with higher quality, predictability, and reliability.


Teams are engaged, accountable, and operating with clear goals.


Stakeholders have confidence in delivery transparency and execution.


Engineering standards, DevOps practices, secure development, API patterns, and AI usage guidelines are consistently applied.


Technical debt, defects, manual effort, and production instability are reduced.


AI adoption is practical, secure, governed, and measurable.






 






What We Offer





 







Competitive salary and performance bonus program


Fully remote work environment


Comprehensive benefits package including 401k match


19 days of PTO per year; 10 holidays per year


Opportunity to work in a mission-driven organization where your work directly shapes clarity, confidence, and better outcomes across healthcare






 






Travel





 






Up to 10% travel, based on business needs.  While our roles are 100% remote, we do get together a few times a year for up to one week.  Employees are given ample notice and attendance is expected. 




 




Work Environment





 






This position operates in a professional work environment and requires the use of standard office equipment such as a computer, keyboard, mouse, webcam, and phone.





 






Equal Opportunity / Affirmative Action Statement





 






Staritas is committed to providing equal employment opportunities to all employees and applicants for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex, national origin, age, disability, genetic information, sexual orientation, gender identity, or veteran status.





 






As a federal contractor, Staritas complies with all applicable federal, state, and local laws regarding nondiscrimination and affirmative action. We are committed to creating a workplace that is fair, inclusive, and grounded in merit-based decision-making.





 






Accommodation Statement





 






If you need reasonable accommodation to apply for this position, please contact the recruiter.




 




Pay Transparency Statement 
 






 






Staritas posts compensation ranges and benefits for all open roles. The compensation range for this position is $155,000 - $185,000, plus performance bonus and a comprehensive benefits package. Compensation offered will depend on factors including experience, skills, geographic location, and internal equity. Staritas does not retaliate against employees or applicants for discussing compensation.





 






Benefits Statement 





 






Staritas offers a comprehensive and competitive benefits package designed to support the health, wellbeing, and financial security of our employees. Benefits include medical, dental, and vision coverage; retirement savings plans with company contributions; paid time off and company holidays; paid parental and family leave; and access to employee assistance and wellness resources. Additional benefits include life and disability insurance, professional development opportunities, and flexible work arrangements. 





 






Learn more about us by visiting www.staritas.com.

Furthermore, 'pragmatic engineering decisions,' 'global backend at scale,' and 'rapid iteration frameworks' from the 'nice to have' section are woven into the competencies, significantly increasing the tailoring density. The summary is concise at 4 sentences, and the competencies list contains exactly 8 highly descriptive, tailored items, ensuring maximum impact within a single-page layout. The refined content demonstrates strong alignment with Second Dinner's values for innovation, quality, and leadership in game development.
summary: Visionary Engineering Leader with over 20 years of experience, adept at building and scaling high-performing software engineering and QA teams for award-winning, large-scale live-service games. Proven expertise as a technical visionholder, executing strategic technical roadmaps, and owning end-to-end delivery pipelines from planning to production to deploy robust applications that provide delightful player experiences. Committed to fostering a collaborative culture of inclusion, balancing aggressive execution with pragmatic engineering decisions and a customer-centric focus on innovation. Expert in operational excellence across cloud-native environments like AWS, ensuring today's engineering decisions enable tomorrow's innovation and long-term solutions.

Eric Garvey, SPHR 

• 3rd

People at Second Dinner

Ask about job
About the job

Who We Are

Second Dinner is an award-winning independent game development studio. Just how many awards, you ask? A whopping 22 in total so far… Including Mobile Game of the Year from both The Game Awards and DICE, Best Strategy Game from IGN, and the prestigious Apple Design Award for Innovation. Yes, that's a lot of awards! But these accolades don't magically materialize; they're a testament to the amazing folks who make up our studio. You could be the next addition to our award-winning team! Continue reading to find out more.

For our debut game, MARVEL SNAP, we've partnered with Marvel (Trust us, we had to pinch ourselves too!). We've got other SUPER FUN (but still secret) projects in the works and this role is a chance to be a part of something new, and special at the company. As a remote-first company, we value diversity and encourage applications from everyone who can bring something unique to the table.

Our Engineering Team

At Second Dinner, we believe that great people in a great work environment will lead us to great games and player experiences. In engineering, we empower our team to build and innovate with best in class tools in ways they haven't been used before. Our goal is to simplify the game development process and redefine what is possible in game development.

Your Role

As a Senior Director of Engineering, you will report to the GM of MARVEL SNAP and your primary responsibility will be to tackle the technical challenges facing the game team. You’ll oversee a large team of talented MARVEL SNAP engineers to develop and improve tools and systems. Your work will empower the entire team to provide our players with delightful and robust gameplay experiences. You are accountable for ensuring that today’s engineering decisions enable tomorrow’s innovation. If you would like to play an integral part in shaping the technology foundations that will support our team and our players for years to come, APPLY!

What You’ll Do


Ensure the best outcomes from our 20-person engineering group and 6-person QA team
Act as technical visionholder for MARVEL SNAP, as we transition to long-term solutions
Own the delivery pipeline from planning to production, remove friction and deliver quality features to players in a timely fashion
Champion pragmatic engineering decisions: know when to build fast and when to build right
In conjunction with Production, ensure quality output from xDev partners, evaluation, onboarding, integration, and accountability
Define quality and deployment standards that scale across internal and xDev teams, then empower others to uphold them
Be the kind of leader people want to work for, hold meaningful 1:1s, track growth, and build actionable career plans
Iterate on hiring, pinch hit when the team needs you, and stay close to the code


What You’ll Need


Proven experience shipping and operating a live-service game
Track record of technical management of external development partners or vendors
Add value at every level: IC engineers, external partners, production, and leadership
Experience leading an engineering team through the ups and downs of long-term live services games
Strong familiarity with Unity, AWS, Git, and .NET
Comfortable both setting strategic direction and rolling up your sleeves


Nice To Have, But Not Necessary


Experience designing and scaling a hub-and-spoke or xDev-heavy engineering model
Experience operating a global backend at scale
Background in live-service game telemetry, A/B testing, or rapid iteration frameworks


The total compensation for this position includes a new hire offer base salary range of $ 270,000-300,000 USD + equity + comprehensive benefits + potential for discretionary performance bonuses.

Individual pay within this salary range may span multiple levels within the discipline and is determined by assessed job-related skills, experience, relevant education or training. It also factors in market demands and business needs. The disclosed range is not adjusted based on location and may be subject to change or modification based on business needs in the future. Your recruiter can answer any questions about new hire total compensation during the hiring process.

An overview of the benefits and perks at Second Dinner:


Medical, Dental, and Vision insurance plans with Second Dinner paying 100% of premiums for employees and 75% for dependents for many plans
401(k) contribution with no waiting period
16 weeks paid parental leave with no waiting period
Home office improvement bonus
Paid Vacation & Sick time
Up to 10 BetterHelp sessions covered each benefits plan year
Remote-first with core overlap hours between 10AM and 4PM PT
Company Summer Holiday shutdown (week of July 4)
Company Winter Holiday shutdown (Dec 25-Jan 1)
Company Events - In-person all-hands gathering, and virtual events throughout the year


We are an equal opportunity employer that places high value on diversity and inclusion. We do not discriminate on the basis of race, color, ancestry, national origin, religion, age, disability status, sex (including pregnancy), gender, gender identity, gender expression, sexual orientation, medical condition, genetic information, marital status, military status, or veteran status.

You must be legally authorized to work and reside in the United States. We do not sponsor visas or support employment outside the U.S. for this role.

Compensation Range: $270K - $300K

Senior Talent Acquisition Professional | Full Cycle Recruitment | Engineering & Information Technology

Job poster

Ask about job
About the job

Come make the world and accelerate your success. 

It takes great people to achieve greatness. People with a sense of purpose and integrity. People with a relentless pursuit of excellence. People who care about making things better For Those Who Make The World™. Sound like you? Join our top-notch team of approximately 43,500 diverse and high-performing professionals globally who are making their mark on some of the world’s most beloved brands, including DEWALT®, BLACK+DECKER®, CRAFTSMAN®, STANLEY®, CUB CADET®, and HUSTLER®.

The Job: 

As a IT Solutions Director you’ll be part of our Corporate Information Technology team working as a remote employee. The IT Solutions Director is a visionary leader responsible for setting and executing the strategic direction for IT solutions and platforms across a global enterprise. This role is pivotal in driving digital transformation and operational excellence by overseeing the entire lifecycle—from selection and implementation to ongoing management—of critical IT solutions. Ensuring tool governance aligns with business objectives, maximizes efficiency, and delivers measurable ROI, while maintaining robust vendor relationships and optimizing cost-effectiveness.

A core accountability is leading technology enablement within the IT operating model transformation, with special emphasis on infusing artificial intelligence capabilities into the IT solutions roadmap. By leveraging deep expertise in AI, enhancing automation, analytics, and innovation, while supporting enterprise-wide business growth and agility. The IT Solutions Director also provides governance and leadership for the ServiceNow enterprise platform and its key modules, ensuring seamless integration, process improvement, and compliance with security and regulatory standards.

This position demands strategic thinking, exceptional leadership, and the ability to manage complex technology ecosystems, ensuring the organization remains at the forefront of technology enablement and operational excellence in the global marketplace.

You’ll get to:



Establish and communicate the strategic roadmap for IT solution selection, implementation, and lifecycle management, ensuring alignment with organizational goals and digital transformation initiatives. 
Define and execute technology enablement strategies as part of the IT operating model transformation, optimizing critical platforms and driving enterprise-wide innovation. 
Lead enterprise-wide technology enablement initiatives, infusing artificial intelligence and automation into the IT solutions roadmap to drive innovation and business growth. 
Stay current with industry trends and emerging practices in product management, agile delivery, and digital transformation, continually evaluating and adopting new technologies to evolve the IT solutions landscape. 
Transform the ServiceNow environment into a robust, scalable enterprise platform, ensuring alignment with global business needs, supporting effective cross-functional collaboration, and establishing governance for key modules (IT Asset Management, IT Service Management, IT Operations Management, HR Service Delivery, Integrated Risk Management, Third-Party Supplier Risk Management) to drive standardization, collaboration, and strong governance practices and platform management. 
Spearhead the creation and governance of a Center of Excellence for SDLC tooling—including Azure DevOps, Jira, and advanced AI tools—setting strategic direction, integrating workflows, and promoting Agile and Product Management best practices. 
Establish the strategic vision for the Power BI platform, ensuring robust data governance, compliance with industry standards, and alignment with enterprise reporting and transformation goals. 
Lead the integration and enablement of Generative AI within Power BI, advancing analytics, automation, and AI-powered reporting to deliver actionable business insights. 
Develop comprehensive training and user enablement programs, fostering a data-driven culture and ensuring high standards of data quality, integrity, and stewardship across platforms. 
Lead governance processes for IT solutions and platforms, ensuring compliance with business objectives, regulatory requirements, and security standards. 
Implement and promote industry standards and frameworks to ensure best-in-class IT solutions governance, risk management, and operational excellence. 
Manage and empower cross-functional teams to drive process improvements, foster innovation, and implement technology solutions that enhance operational efficiency and agility. 
Oversee vendor relationships, negotiate contracts, and optimize IT solutions usage for cost-effectiveness and performance. 
Provide executive-level reporting and insights on tool performance, ROI, and digital transformation progress, leveraging data analytics and industry benchmarking. 
Oversee the selection, deployment, and continuous improvement of project and portfolio management tools and IT investment management solutions to maximize value delivery and align technology investments with business objectives. 
Drive change management and adoption initiatives, ensuring successful deployment of new tools and processes and fostering continuous improvement. 



The Person: 

You love to learn and grow and be acknowledged for your valuable contributions. You’re not intimidated by innovation. Wouldn’t it be great if you could do your job and do a world of good? In fact, you embrace it. You also have:

Required Skills



Strategic IT roadmap development and execution 
Technology enablement and digital transformation leadership 
AI integration and advanced analytics implementation 
IT solution lifecycle management (selection, deployment, optimization) 
ServiceNow platform governance and module management (ITAM, ITOM, ITSM, IRM, TPSRM, HRSD) 
SDLC tooling expertise (Azure DevOps, Jira, Agile, Product Management) 
Data governance, quality, and compliance (ISO, GDPR, industry standards) 
Power BI platform management and AI-powered reporting 
Vendor management and contract negotiation 
Cross-functional team leadership and collaboration 
Change management and user adoption facilitation 
Executive reporting, ROI analysis, and value delivery 
Continuous improvement and innovation evaluation 
Risk management and operational excellence frameworks 
Results Orientation and Accountability 
Problem Management / RCA 
Oral and Written Communications 



Education/Experience



Undergraduate degree and 10+ project management experience, or 
Graduate degree and 8-10 of years experience in IT Tools management or a related discipline 
Demonstrated leadership in the management IT tools and cross-functional teams. 
Proven expertise in IT Tools Management. 



The Details: 

You’ll receive a competitive salary and a great benefits plan, including:



Medical, dental, life, vision, disability, 401(k), Employee Stock Purchase Plan, paid time off, and tuition reimbursement in addition to programs & benefits in support of your well-being. 
Discounts on Stanley Black & Decker tools and other partner programs. 



And More: 

We want our company to be a place you’ll want to be – and stay. Being part of our team means you’ll get to:



Grow: Be part of our global company with 20+ brands to grow and develop your skills along multiple career paths. 
Learn: Have access to a wealth of learning resources, including our digital learning portal. 
Belong: Experience an awesome place to work, where we have mutual respect and a great appreciation for a wide range of perspectives and experiences. 
Give Back: Help us continue to make positive changes locally and globally through volunteerism, giving back, and sustainable business practices. 



What’s more, you’ll get that pride that comes from empowering makers, doers, protectors, and everyday heroes all over the world. We’re more than the #1 tools company. More than a driving force in outdoor power equipment. More than a global leader in industrial. We’re visionaries and innovators. As successful as we’ve been in the past, we have so much further to go. That’s where you come in. Join us!

The Salary range for this position is $134,300.00 - $231,700.00



This is the lowest to highest annual full-time salary range we would pay for this role at the time of this posting. The salary offered within the range may vary depending on multiple factors including job level, geographic location, job-related knowledge, skills, qualifications, experience and in compliance with local wage requirements. Please note that salary is only one component of total rewards at Stanley Black & Decker. 
The salary range listed in this job posting reflects the range for the primary location specified. The salary range may vary for other locations. 
The successful candidate may be eligible for annual merit increases. 
Medical, dental, life, vision, disability,401(k), Employee Stock Purchase Plan, paid time off, and tuition reimbursement in addition to programs & benefits in support of your well-being. 
Discounts on Stanley Black & Decker tool sand other partner programs. 



We Don’t Just Build The World, We Build Innovative Technology Too.

Joining the Stanley Black & Decker team means working in an innovative, tech-driven and highly collaborative team environment supported by over 43,500 professionals in 60 countries across the globe. Here, you’ll get the unique chance to impact some of the world’s most iconic brands including STANLEY TOOLS, DEWALT, CRAFTSMAN, MAC TOOLS and Black + Decker. Your ideas and solutions have the potential to reach millions of customers as we work together to write the next chapter in our history. Come build with us and take your career to new heights.

Who We Are
We’re the World’s largest tool company. We’re industry visionaries. We’re solving problems and advancing the manufacturing trade through innovative technology and our Industry 4.0 Initiative. We are committed to ensuring our state-of-the-art “smart factory” products and services provide greater quality to our customers & greater environmental and social value to our planet. We are unique in that we have a rich and storied history dating back to 1843, but that hasn't stopped us from evolving into a vibrant, diverse, global growth company.

What You’ll Also Get
Career Opportunity: Career paths aren’t linear here. Being part of our global company with 60+ brands gives you the chance to grow and develop your skills along multiple career paths.

Benefits & Perks
You’ll get a competitive salary and a comprehensive benefits plan that includes medical, dental, life, vision, wellness program, disability, retirement benefits, Employee Stock Purchase Plan, Paid Time Off, including paid vacation, holidays & personal days, and tuition reimbursement. And, of course, discounts on Stanley Black & Decker tools and products and well as discount programs for many other vendors and partners.

Learning & Development: 

Our lifelong learning philosophy means you’ll have access to a wealth of state-of-the-art learning resources, including our Lean Academy and online university (where you can get certificates and specializations from renowned colleges and universities).

Diverse & Inclusive Culture:

We pride ourselves on being an awesome place to work. We respect and embrace differences because that’s how the best work gets done. You’ll find we like to have fun here, too.

Purpose-Driven Company:

You’ll help us continue to make positive changes in the local communities where we work and live as well as in the broader world through volunteerism, giving back and sustainable business practices.

EEO Statement:

All qualified applicants to Stanley Black & Decker are considered for employment without regard to race, color, religion, age, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, disability, veteran’s status or any other protected characteristic.

If you require reasonable accommodation to complete an application or access our website, please contact us at (860) 827-3923 or at accommodations@sbdinc.com. Due to volume, we cannot respond to unrelated inquiries about the status of a completed application or resetting an account password.

Know Your Rights: Workplace discrimination is illegal (eeoc.gov)

Proven expertise in owning core platforms such as POS, CRM, websites, and call centers, leading strategic system planning, and executing comprehensive release cycles. Neighborly®

Director of Systems

Full-time

About the job

Are you looking for a place where you can bring your leadership, technical systems expertise, and project management skills?

Welcome to Neighborly—the hub of service brands that connects customers to top-notch local experts who repair, maintain, and enhance homes and businesses. Our long-standing business, with over 40 years of experience, is focused on strategic innovation as we build the future of home and business services. Bring your ambition to the table as we unlock new doors together, taking your career to the next level.

Bring Your Experience And Be Empowered To Innovate.

As a Director of Systems supporting the Five Star Painting brand, you will:


Own Core Platforms: Drive performance and reliability for POS, CRM, websites, and call centers. 
Strategy & Roadmap: Lead system planning, release cycles, and long-term improvements. 
Support & Escalation: Act as the primary lead for critical system issues and resolutions. 
Partnerships: Collaborate with tech, vendors, and ops to drive tool adoption and optimization. 
Standardization: Streamline tools and processes to eliminate duplication across platforms. 
Data & Insights: Build dashboards and reporting tools for data-driven decision-making. 
Vendor Management: Manage external partners to ensure quality and aligned priorities. 
Training & Expertise: Provide subject-matter expertise and training to franchisees and internal teams. 


(Required Qualifications)

Bring your skills and be inspired to achieve success.


Experience:
5+ Years: Progressive leadership in franchise or multi-location systems. 
Operations Tech: Expertise in managing vendor relations, system integrations, and operational workflows. 
Project Management: Proven track record in strategic road mapping and cross-functional coordination. 
AI Savvy: Familiarity with emerging AI applications (preferred). 


Skills: 
Proven experience building relationships
Clear, confident communication and strong relationship-building skills. 
Proven leadership experience in a franchise-based or multi-unit environment. 
Deep expertise in system lifecycle management (POS, CRM, ERP, call center platforms, etc.). 
Ability to diagnose complex system issues and drive long-term solutions. 
Analytical mindset with the ability to build dashboards, identify trends, and support data-driven decisions. 
Ability to thrive in a fast-paced, evolving environment with multiple priorities. 
A positive, adaptable attitude—and a sense of humor. 
Education: Bachelor’s degree required; Master’s or MBA highly preferred. 
Schedule / in-office requirements: 
Travel Required (25%)
(Hybrid working model; Mon-Wed in office, Thur/Fri from home)
Our office locations are:
500 E John Carpenter Fwy, Irving, TX
1010 N University Parks Drive, Waco, TX

Bring your goals and be enabled to reach them. 


Competitive Pay: Commensurate with experience
Schedule: Full-time, Monday – Friday, 8 AM – 5 PM local time zone
Benefits: Check out our benefits offerings here: Neighborly Benefits
Financial Benefits: Equity and bonus opportunities


Neighborly® is fully committed to Equal Employment Opportunity and to attracting, retaining, developing and promoting the most qualified employees without regard to their race, gender, color, religion, sexual orientation, national origin, age, physical or mental disability, citizenship status, veteran status, or any other characteristic prohibited by federal or state law. We are dedicated to providing a work environment free from discrimination and harassment, and where employees are treated with respect and dignity and encouraged to be their authentic self.

Not the right opportunity for you? 

Share this job with a friend and follow us on LinkedIn for future opportunity updates.

analysis: The draft resume elements were already exceptionally well-tailored to the LTK Engineering Manager role, demonstrating deep alignment with the job description's specific requirements. The summary effectively encapsulates over 20 years of software experience, a 2+ year management tenure, and a specialized focus on full-stack teams (iOS, Web, Backend), rapid releases, and cloud environments, particularly AWS. It explicitly integrates the 'creator growth and onboarding' domain, 'experimentation,' 'data-driven optimization,' and 'enterprise AI tooling,' which are critical to LTK's strategic initiatives. The competencies list is robust, with exactly eight distinct, concise items that directly address every key technical skill (AWS services, Docker, CI/CD, APIs) and leadership qualification (people leadership, agile delivery, cross-functional relationships, talent development) outlined in the job description. All irrelevant jargon, like 'clinical,' has been successfully removed, ensuring a focused and professional presentation. The refined content maximizes keyword density while maintaining a natural, executive-grade tone, positioning the candidate as an ideal fit for this role at LTK.
summary: Visionary, results-driven Engineering Leader with over 20 years of software development experience and a proven 2+ year track record in engineering management, specializing in leading and scaling high-performing full-stack teams across iOS, Web, and Backend domains. Expertly translate strategic technical roadmaps into rapid, continuous releases, driving the entire product lifecycle from conceptualization through scalable production deployment in cloud environments, particularly AWS. Deeply committed to fostering a culture of experimentation and data-driven optimization for user-facing features, while strategically integrating enterprise AI tooling to enhance efficiency and maintain competitive advantage for creator growth and onboarding experiences. My leadership journey emphasizes building collaborative teams, nurturing talent, and delivering impactful, customer-centric solutions.
competencies:
\item \textbf{Engineering Leadership and Technical Vision:} Guiding full-stack teams in developing robust features, defining technical vision, and collaborating with cross-functional peers to align with business objectives.
\item \textbf{Enterprise AI Tooling and Innovation:} Driving integration and experimentation of enterprise AI tooling, discovering novel applications, and measuring efficiency gains to foster continuous improvement.
\item \textbf{Agile Delivery and Product Lifecycle Management:} Owning the delivery process, leading Agile ceremonies, and ensuring rapid, continuous releases in CI/CD environments for high-visibility features.
\item \textbf{Scalable Cloud Architectures (AWS):} Architecting and deploying highly scalable distributed systems in AWS, leveraging services such as ECS, Lambda, RDS, Redshift, and Glue within Dockerized environments.
\item \textbf{People Leadership and Talent Development:} Proven history of leading, hiring, coaching, and performance managing developers, fostering growth and minimizing retention risks across full-stack teams.
\item \textbf{Full-Stack Development and User Experience:} Leading full-stack squads (iOS, Web, Backend) to build user-facing, high-visibility features that enhance creator growth and onboarding experiences.
\item \textbf{Data-Driven Experimentation and Optimization:} Cultivating a culture of experimentation, leveraging A/B testing and data analytics to optimize creator onboarding and reporting funnels.
\item \textbf{API Architecture and Integration:} Expertise in building and maintaining highly scalable internal and public-facing APIs, including RESTful, JSON, and HTTP protocols.

About the job

Position Title: Manager, Engineering - Creator Growth & Onboarding

Location: Remote from the U.S. 

About the role

We are searching for an engineering leader to manage a fullstack squad (iOS, Web, Backend) responsible for the user experiences and systems used to onboard and drive creator growth across LTK, one of the largest creator-guided shopping and brand marketing platforms in the industry! As the leader of one of our development teams, you will be responsible for conducting, leading and coordinating software development activities throughout the product lifecycle as well as managing the day-to-day activities of the development team within an Agile Development environment.

How you will make an impact

Provide technical and people leadership to guide your team’s development, reviews, and maintenance of new features, product requirements documents, technical design documents and functional specifications
Influence the technical vision for your department through discussions with XFN peers and senior management.
Owns the delivery process and Agile ceremonies, ensuring proper backlog refinement and value delivery in every sprint.
Partner with senior management to identify appropriate metrics to monitor team performance.
Drive a culture of experimentation, leveraging A/B testing and data analytics to validate hypotheses and optimize the creator onboarding and reporting funnels.
Take an active role in hiring, goal setting, performance management, promotions, and retention strategies across your team.
Minimize retention risks and create effective development plans to prepare employees for their next role.
You focus on growing leaders – emphasizing all aspects of the employee lifecycle (recruiting, onboarding, performance management, and employee development). 
Work with the team and stakeholders to break down and prioritize work to reduce technical debt.
Identify and pursue improvements in engineering and organizational process, gathering and using data to support and guide continuous growth in product excellence and engineering productivity.
Drive integration and experimentation of enterprise AI tooling across your team’s workflows to realize new efficiencies and help LTK maintain a competitive advantage.

What you will bring to LTK

Leadership Qualifications:

At least 2+ years in an engineering management role with a proven and consistent history of leading, hiring, coaching, and performance management of developers.
Experience managing a fullstack team.
Proven experience in rapid, continuous releases, used to shipping multiple times a day 
Able to drive productivity and motivate a team to full project completion
Detail oriented, with proven methods for tracking project delivery and managing-up progress
Proven ability to mentor and grow engineers across diverse technical backgrounds
Ability to build, maintain, and influence XFN product and design relationships to accomplish team objectives.

Technical Skills:

8+ years experience with software development, architectures and infrastructure.
Demonstrates consistent utilization of AI within daily workflows and actively experiments to discover novel applications to improve your reach.

About the job

Position Title: Manager, Engineering - Creator Growth & Onboarding

Location: Remote from the U.S. 

About the role

We are searching for an engineering leader to manage a fullstack squad (iOS, Web, Backend) responsible for the user experiences and systems used to onboard and drive creator growth across LTK, one of the largest creator-guided shopping and brand marketing platforms in the industry! As the leader of one of our development teams, you will be responsible for conducting, leading and coordinating software development activities throughout the product lifecycle as well as managing the day-to-day activities of the development team within an Agile Development environment.

How you will make an impact

Provide technical and people leadership to guide your team’s development, reviews, and maintenance of new features, product requirements documents, technical design documents and functional specifications
Influence the technical vision for your department through discussions with XFN peers and senior management.
Owns the delivery process and Agile ceremonies, ensuring proper backlog refinement and value delivery in every sprint.
Partner with senior management to identify appropriate metrics to monitor team performance.
Drive a culture of experimentation, leveraging A/B testing and data analytics to validate hypotheses and optimize the creator onboarding and reporting funnels.
Take an active role in hiring, goal setting, performance management, promotions, and retention strategies across your team.
Minimize retention risks and create effective development plans to prepare employees for their next role.
You focus on growing leaders – emphasizing all aspects of the employee lifecycle (recruiting, onboarding, performance management, and employee development). 
Work with the team and stakeholders to break down and prioritize work to reduce technical debt.
Identify and pursue improvements in engineering and organizational process, gathering and using data to support and guide continuous growth in product excellence and engineering productivity.
Drive integration and experimentation of enterprise AI tooling across your team’s workflows to realize new efficiencies and help LTK maintain a competitive advantage.

What you will bring to LTK

Leadership Qualifications:

At least 2+ years in an engineering management role with a proven and consistent history of leading, hiring, coaching, and performance management of developers.
Experience managing a fullstack team.
Proven experience in rapid, continuous releases, used to shipping multiple times a day 
Able to drive productivity and motivate a team to full project completion
Detail oriented, with proven methods for tracking project delivery and managing-up progress
Proven ability to mentor and grow engineers across diverse technical backgrounds
Ability to build, maintain, and influence XFN product and design relationships to accomplish team objectives.

Technical Skills:

8+ years experience with software development, architectures and infrastructure.
Demonstrates consistent utilization of AI within daily workflows and actively experiments to discover novel applications to improve your reach.
Measures efficiency and quality gains from AI adoption, drives the feedback loop for continuous improvement based on the results.
Proven track record of delivering user-facing, high-visibility features
Deep expertise in at least one stack (Backend, Web, or iOS) with a working knowledge and the ability to influence direction across the others.
History of building highly scalable distributed systems and processes in a cloud environment
Experience working in a Dockerized environment.
Experience in building products and features in CI/CD environments.
Experience with AWS services such as ECS, Lambda, RDS, Redshift, Glue (among others).
Have built scalable internal and public facing APIs (RESTful, JSON, HTTP)

Nice to have: Have built AI powered products, knowledge of affiliate advertising space, onboarding flows, user performance analytics and reporting.

 What we offer
The opportunity to be part of the leading global company in creator commerce
A remote-first, productivity-first environment
Competitive compensation and benefits package to meet the needs of you and your family
401(k) with LTK company matching
Medical Insurance, Vision Insurance, Dental Insurance
Paid Maternity Leave and Paid Paternity Leave
Summer Fridays and Flexible PTO

summary: A visionary Senior Engineering Leader with over 20 years of robust software experience, adept at guiding high-impact teams in designing and delivering scalable, reliable, and cross-cutting systems spanning multiple product domains. Proven expertise in accelerating AI-powered initiatives, translating complex business objectives into clear technical roadmaps, and driving product excellence for customer-facing platforms at scale. competencies: \item \textbf{People and Team Leadership:} Expertly cultivating high-performing engineering teams through mentorship, outcome-driven professional development, and fostering an inclusive culture of ownership and psychological safety.\item \textbf{Scalable Systems Architecture:} Guiding the design, evolution, and operation of complex, distributed systems at scale, ensuring rigorous standards for reliability, performance, security, and maintainability.\item \textbf{AI-Powered Initiative Leadership:} Accelerating investments in AI-powered and data-driven systems, translating advanced ML/LLM concepts into production-grade, high-impact customer solutions.\item \textbf{Technical Judgment and Governance:} Providing senior technical leadership, diving deep into architectural discussions, reviewing designs, and skillfully navigating complex trade-offs to ensure platform evolution.\item \textbf{Execution and Delivery Ownership:} Owning end-to-end delivery outcomes across discovery to production, building clear technical roadmaps, driving clarity in ambiguity, and making timely decisions.\item \textbf{Modern Engineering Practices:} Championing robust Git, CI/CD, automated testing, observability, and performance monitoring strategies to uphold engineering quality and operational excellence.\item \textbf{Cross-Functional Collaboration:} Partnering effectively with Product, Design, Data, and other Engineering teams to deliver cohesive, end-to-end solutions, optimize speed, and maximize impact.\item \textbf{Cloud-Native and Python Ecosystems:} Architecting and integrating solutions within leading cloud-native ML ecosystems (Azure, AWS) and proficiently leveraging Python for large-scale system development.

About the job

Ready to be a Titan?

The Engineering Leadership team at ServiceTitan is responsible for growing the organization and delivering product excellence. We provide exciting opportunities to make an immediate impact in our rapidly growing startup. We are quality minded, use the most modern tools on the Microsoft .NET platform, have an amazing culture, and love to solve complex problems. Over 50,000 people depend on our software to perform all their daily activities.

As we accelerate our investments in AI-powered initiatives, we are looking for a Senior Engineering Manager who can lead high-impact teams, operate effectively in ambiguity, and guide the design and delivery of scalable, cross-cutting systems that span multiple domains.

This role is ideal for a senior leader who combines strong people leadership with deep technical judgment, and who thrives in environments where speed, clarity, and accountability matter.

What You’ll Do: 

People & Team Leadership



Establish clear, outcome-driven professional development plans with milestones for engineers and managers.
Provide ongoing coaching through 1:1s, feedback, and active mentorship, raising the bar on both technical and leadership excellence.
Build a strong team culture grounded in ownership, trust, psychological safety, and continuous improvement.
Attract, hire, and retain top engineering talent in close partnership with Recruiting.
Grow future technical and people leaders by creating opportunities for increased scope, ownership, and impact.


Technical & Systems Leadership



Guide teams in the design, evolution, and operation of scalable, reliable, and maintainable systems, especially in areas spanning multiple services and product domains.
Act as a senior technical leader who can dive deep into architecture discussions, review designs, and help teams navigate complex technical trade-offs.
Ensure high standards of engineering quality, including system reliability, performance, security, and operational excellence.
Balance short-term delivery needs with long-term technical health, actively managing technical debt and platform evolution.


Execution & Delivery



Own delivery outcomes for your teams across discovery, design, implementation, and production.
Build, communicate, and continuously refine a clear technical and execution roadmap aligned with business and product goals.
Drive clarity in ambiguous problem spaces by breaking down complex initiatives into actionable plans with measurable outcomes.
Identify risks early, unblock teams, and make timely decisions to maintain momentum in fast-moving environments.


Cross-Functional & Cross-Team Collaboration



Partner closely with Product, Design, Data, and other Engineering teams to deliver cohesive, end-to-end solutions.
Coordinate effectively across multiple teams and stakeholders, aligning dependencies and resolving conflicts to optimize for speed and impact.
Communicate clearly and regularly with senior leaders, providing crisp updates on progress, risks, and trade-offs.
Foster strong collaboration across ServiceTitan, especially when working on initiatives that cut across existing systems and ownership boundaries.


 What You’ll Bring:



7+ years of experience managing software engineering teams, including senior engineers and/or managers.
Strong technical background with experience designing and operating complex, distributed systems used at scale.
Proven ability to lead teams through ambiguity, balancing urgency with sound technical decision-making.
Experience coordinating work across multiple teams, domains, or systems, aligning stakeholders toward shared outcomes.
Excellent written and verbal communication skills, with the ability to translate complex technical topics to diverse audiences.
Demonstrated track record of delivering high-impact, business-critical initiatives end-to-end.


Ideal Candidates Also Have



Proficiency in one or more of the following languages: C#, Java, Python, or Node.js/TypeScript (full stack only).
Familiarity with modern engineering practices: Git, CI/CD, automated testing, observability, and performance monitoring.
Exposure to AI-enabled or data-driven systems, or a strong interest in building intelligent, customer-facing platforms.
Experience operating in fast-paced environments where speed, ownership, and adaptability are critical.


Be Human With Us:

Being human isn’t about checking every box on a list. It’s about the experiences we have, people we meet, and the perspectives we share. So, if you have the skills but are hesitant to apply because of your background, apply anyway. We need amazing people like you to help us challenge the conventional and think differently about the problems that we’re solving. We’re in this together. Come be human, with us.

Use of AI Technology:

We use technology, including automated and AI-assisted tools, to support certain aspects of our recruitment process. These tools are designed to improve efficiency and enhance the candidate experience. AI tools are not used to make hiring decisions; all hiring decisions are made by our hiring teams.

What We Offer:

When you join our team, you’re not just accepting a job. You’re making a career move. Here’s how we’ll support you in doing some of the most impactful work of your career:



Flextime, recognition, and support for autonomous work: Flexible time off with ample learning and development opportunities to continue growing your career. We offer a comprehensive onboarding program, leadership training for Titans at all levels, and other programs and events. Great work is rewarded through Bonusly, peer-nominated awards, and more.
Holistic health and wellness benefits: Company-paid medical, dental, and vision (with 100% employer paid options and 90% coverage for dependents), FSA and HSA, 401k match, and telehealth options including memberships to One Medical.
Support for Titans at all stages of life: Parental leave and support, up to $20k in fertility services (i.e. IUI and IVF), surrogacy, and adoption reimbursement, on demand maternity support through Maven Maternity, free breast milk shipping through Maven Milk, pet insurance, legal advisory services, financial planning tools, and more.


At ServiceTitan, we celebrate individuality and uniqueness. We believe that the convergence of fresh perspectives and experiences from all walks of life is what makes our product and culture so great. We strongly encourage people from underrepresented groups to apply. We do not discriminate against employees based on race, color, religion, sex, national origin, gender identity or expression, age, disability, pregnancy (including childbirth, breastfeeding, or related medical condition), genetic information, protected military or veteran status, sexual orientation, or any other characteristic protected by applicable federal, state or local laws.

ServiceTitan is committed to fair and equitable compensation for all of our employees. We thoughtfully consider a wide range of factors when determining individual compensation, which may change over time. We comply with all applicable minimum wage laws. For candidates in the United States, the good faith salary ranges estimate for this role is Zone 1: $218,300 USD - $327,500 USD Applicable for: CA, CT, DC, MD, MA, NJ, NY, VA, and WA Zone 2: $204,000 USD - $306,000 USD Applicable for: All other US locations. International Compensation for candidates residing outside the United States will vary by location and will be discussed during the hiring process. Actual compensation within a range is determined by factors including relevant experience, skill set, qualifications, and performance. In addition to base salary, our total compensation package includes an annual bonus, equity, and a holistic suite of benefits.

About the job

Overview

Cotiviti is seeking a Director of Engineering to lead multiple software engineering teams within our Payment Integrity business. In this role, you will provide strategic and technical leadership across one or more product domains, leading through engineering managers and team leads to deliver high-quality, reliable software solutions.

You will champion an AI-native approach to software delivery by integrating AI tools, automation, and intelligent engineering workflows across the software development lifecycle to accelerate delivery, improve quality, and increase engineering effectiveness. You will partner closely with product management, enterprise data management, IT, and business stakeholders to align technology delivery with business priorities while meeting healthcare compliance, security, and performance requirements.

This role requires a hands-on, lead-by-example leader who sets the standard for AI-native engineering and shows teams how modern software delivery can be transformed through AI, automation, and strong technical judgment.

Responsibilities


 Leadership & Strategy: Build and lead a multi-team engineering organization with clear goals, outcome-based metrics, and a culture of continuous improvement. 
 Resource Optimization: Improve delivery throughput and resource utilization through streamlined processes, automation, and AI-enabled tools without compromising quality or maintainability. 
 AI-Driven Development: Advance AI-enabled engineering practices by integrating AI tools and automation into workflows with human oversight and clear guardrails. 
 Technical Execution & Oversight: Lead roadmap execution with hands-on technical and architectural oversight to ensure scalable, secure, and maintainable solutions. 
 Technology Alignment: Align teams to enterprise architecture, governance, and shared engineering standards to improve consistency and code quality. 
 Cross-Functional Collaboration: Partner across product, program, operations, and business teams to translate priorities into engineering plans that deliver measurable outcomes. 
 Process Improvement & Innovation: Modernize development and testing practices through automation, DevOps, and AI-enabled workflows that improve quality, security, and efficiency. 
 Talent Development: Recruit, develop, and retain strong engineering talent while building a culture of growth, accountability, and innovation. 
 Complete all responsibilities as outlined in the annual performance review and/or goal setting. 
 Complete all special projects and other duties as assigned. 
 Must be able to perform duties with or without reasonable accommodation. 


Qualifications


 Education & Experience: 8+ years of software engineering experience, including 3–5 years leading engineering teams as a people manager. Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science or a related field required; Master’s degree preferred. 
 Technical Proficiency: Extensive experience designing and delivering complex software systems using Java, React/Angular, microservices, relational and NoSQL databases, cloud platforms, and modern AI-enabled engineering practices, with recent hands-on technical work. 
 Enterprise Software Delivery: Proven success leading multiple teams and managers to deliver enterprise software across distributed organizations. 
 Leadership Skills: Strong leadership skills, including building, developing, and mentoring effective engineering teams. 
 Adaptive Delivery Models: Strong understanding of Lean, iterative, and flow-based delivery practices that support quality, continuous improvement, and effective execution. 
 Modern Engineering Practices: Track record of advancing engineering practices through automated testing, TDD, and CI/CD. 
 Operational & DevOps Knowledge: Solid understanding of software operations and DevOps practices, including reliability, monitoring, telemetry, and incident response. 
 Problem Solving: Strong problem-solving skills and sound judgment in complex technical environments. 
 Strategic Execution: Ability to set and execute short- and long-term goals across projects and teams. 
 Communication: Strong verbal and written communication skills, with the ability to explain technical concepts clearly to diverse stakeholders. 
 Industry & Compliance: Experience in healthcare or other regulated industries with strong compliance, security, and data privacy requirements is preferred. 


Mental Requirements


 Communicating with others to exchange information. 
 Problem-solving and thinking critically. 
 Completing tasks independently. 
 Interpreting data. 
 Making timely decisions in the context of a workflow. 
 Maintaining focus. 
 Assessing the accuracy, neatness and thoroughness of the work assigned. 
 Learning new tasks and completing tasks in situations that have a speed or productivity quota. 
 Remembering and adhering to processes and protocols. 


Physical Requirements And Working Conditions


 Remaining in a stationary position, often standing or sitting for prolonged periods. 
 Repeating motions that may include the wrists, hands, and/or fingers. 
 Must be able to provide a dedicated, secure work area. 
 Must be able to provide high-speed internet access/connectivity and office setup and maintenance. 
 No adverse environmental conditions expected. 


 Base compensation ranges from $206,000 to $247,000 per year. Specific offers are determined by various factors, such as experience, education, skills, certifications, and other business needs. This role is eligible for discretionary bonus consideration. 

 Cotiviti offers team members a competitive benefits package to address a wide range of personal and family needs, including medical, dental, vision, disability, and life insurance coverage, 401(k) savings plans, paid family leave, 9 paid holidays per year, and 17-27 days of Paid Time Off (PTO) per year, depending on specific level and length of service with Cotiviti. For information about our benefits package, please refer to our Careers page. 

 This role is based remotely and all interviews will be conducted virtually. 

Date of posting:00/00/2026

Applications are assessed on a rolling basis. We anticipate that the application window will close on 00/00/2026, but the application window may change depending on the volume of applications received or close immediately if a qualified candidate is selected.

analysis: The draft was extensively optimized to align with the Daktronics 'Director of Software Portfolio Strategy' job description. All irrelevant AI/ML-specific jargon from the base profile was meticulously removed. Key phrases such as 'control systems,' 'Software Business Development, Sales, Services, and Finance' (explicitly listing out all cross-functional teams), 'product vs platform tradeoffs,' 'large installed hardware/software customer bases,' and 'influencing executive decisions' were strategically woven into both the summary and competencies. The summary now serves as a robust 5-line narrative, and the competencies section precisely features 8 items, each concise and heavily keyword-dense, ensuring maximal impact and ATS compliance within the strict one-page constraint. The refined content demonstrates deep expertise in platform strategy, SaaS transformation, and cross-functional leadership, directly mirroring the employer's needs.
summary: Visionary leader with over 20 years of extensive software experience, specializing in comprehensive software portfolio strategy across control systems and SaaS offerings. I excel at setting strategic roadmaps, prioritizing investment across multi-product portfolios, and developing resilient cloud and cloud-edge platforms for scale and customer value. My journey focuses on translating customer and market insights into actionable strategies, fostering robust cross-functional alignment across Product Management, Engineering, Software Business Development, Sales, Services, and Finance. I am dedicated to connecting strategy to execution, influencing executive decisions, and guiding successful SaaS and cloud transformations, modernizing legacy systems for large installed hardware/software customer bases.

About the job

Position Summary

When you work at Daktronics, you’ll be part of something amazing. We design, engineer, manufacture, and support bold, eye-catching digital LED display technology, audio systems, and the software that enables our customers to create and deliver experiences that engage, connect, and entertain audiences.

Our products bring excitement to professional, college and high school games. They attract customers for businesses around the world, and even provide direction for people as they travel, work, and play.

As part of the Daktronics team, you’ll have interesting, impactful work with flexible opportunities. You can learn and grow at a company that provides competitive compensation and meaningful benefits – and the people are second to none.

What will the work look like for me?

We’re seeking a visionary Director of Software Portfolio Strategy to shape the future of our software business and lead platform strategy across software, control systems, and SaaS offerings.

In this role, you will drive portfolio direction, roadmaps, and investment priorities to accelerate growth and build scalable platforms that deliver customer value.

Working across Product Management, Engineering, Software Business Development, Sales, Services, and Finance, you will connect strategy and execution to drive innovation, alignment, and results.

Software Portfolio Strategy Ownership


 Set the software portfolio vision and roadmap. 
 Prioritize products, platforms, and partnerships that drive growth. 


Platform Strategy & Shared Services Alignment


 Define the shared cloud platform strategy. 
 Build scalable, reusable shared services for SaaS growth. 


Cloud–Edge Strategy Definition


 Define the cloud-edge strategy for scale and resilience. 
 Partner with Engineering and Architecture on hybrid solutions. 


Customer, Market, & Strategic Input


 Turn customer and market insight into strategy. 
 Shape roadmap decisions with Product Management. 


SaaS & Business Model Strategy (Influence)


 Influence SaaS packaging, pricing, and growth strategy. 
 Identify growth opportunities across the software lifecycle. 


Investment Prioritization & Governance


 Lead portfolio governance and investment prioritization. 
 Guide investment decisions across the portfolio lifecycle. 


Cross-Functional Alignment & Leadership


 Lead cross-functional alignment across key teams. 
 Help shape executive decisions on strategy and priorities. 


Where is this opportunity located? 

Position has opportunity to work from a U.S. based home office. This position is open to candidates eligible to work in the U.S.

What are the pay and perks?

The typical hiring range for this position is $180,000 to $210,000 annually. The actual offer will consider a wide range of factors, including relevant education, experiences, location, and applicable local legislation.

Information about Daktronics benefits can be found on our website Career Opportunities (www.daktronics.com/en-us/employment/careers) . This information is intended to be a general overview and may be modified by Daktronics at any time.

Qualifications

To be considered for the Director of Software Portfolio Strategy, we require the following:


 Bachelor’s degree, preferably in Engineering or Business. 
 10–15+ years of experience in software strategy or portfolio leadership. 
 Strong experience with multi-product software portfolios and platform-based architectures. 
 Proven understanding of:
 SaaS and cloud transformation. 
 Platform strategy and shared service models. 
 Product vs platform tradeoffs. 
 Demonstrated ability to influence and align across Engineering, Product, and Commercial teams. 
 Strategic thinker with the ability to translate vision into actionable priorities. 
 Preferred Experiences:
 Experience in hybrid cloud + edge environments (e.g., IoT, industrial systems, distributed control environments). 
 Familiarity with:
 Data platforms, telemetry systems, or device ecosystems. 
 Large installed hardware/software customer bases. 
 Experience modernizing legacy software portfolios into unified platforms. 
 Exposure to SaaS transitions or recurring revenue business models. 
 The selected individual must be able to travel by air and ground and comply with the Daktronics Driver Eligibility Policy to begin and maintain employment. This requirement will be verified through a motor vehicle record (MVR) check conducted in accordance with applicable federal, state, provincial, and local laws.
 Ability to travel domestically or internationally, up to a week at a time to customer sites, installations, vendors, conventions, company headquarters, etc. 
 Anticipated travel frequency is 6-12 trips per year or as needed. Must be able to obtain the appropriate visas to travel to other countries as needed. 
 Effectively exercise discretion and independent judgment. 
 Fluent in English, written and verbal. 
 Applicants must be 18 years of age or older. 
 Daktronics does not sponsor, renew, or extend immigration visas for this position. 

Ready to make an impact? Apply now and start your journey with Daktronics. 

Daktronics is an equal opportunity employer. Qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, disability, protected veteran status or other characteristics protected by law.
