competencies: \item \textbf{AI Transformation and Strategy:} Expert at translating executive business objectives into executable engineering roadmaps, leading AI-assisted transformations, and defining architectural vision for enterprise ML systems.\item \textbf{Advanced AI and Agent Architectures:} Deep technical expertise across LLM technologies, including prompt engineering, Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG), vector embeddings, fine-tuning, and multi-agent systems with tool orchestration.\item \textbf{Full SDLC and MLOps Excellence:} End-to-end ownership from research and rapid prototyping through production deployment, ensuring reproducibility, automated testing, and modern software development best practices.\item \textbf{Cloud-Native AI and Scalable Architectures:} Advanced proficiency with cloud-native ML ecosystems across Azure, AWS, and GCP, architecting micro-services and scalable data architectures for enterprise solutions.\item \textbf{Engineering Leadership and Mentorship:} Dedicated to cultivating high-performing, inclusive engineering teams, mentoring developers in AI tools and best practices to drive productivity and technical excellence.\item \textbf{Client Engagement and Technical Consulting:} Proven experience delivering technical guidance to external clients, leading ideation processes, and defining innovative solutions in a consulting capacity.\item \textbf{Modern Software Development Practices:} Expertise in championing automated testing, CI/CD, object-oriented programming, and micro-service API development (RESTful, GraphQL, gRPC) for robust systems.\item \textbf{AI Model Performance and Optimization:} Proficient in optimizing LLM inference latency, cost, and evaluating model performance across reasoning quality, hallucination rate, and task success for enterprise applications.
jobDescription:
General Information
Job Title 
Expert Senior Manager, Engineering Excellence
Job ID 
104886
Work Areas 
Technology & Engineering
Employment Type 
Permanent Full-Time
Location(s) 
Atlanta, Austin, Boston, Chicago, Dallas, Houston, Los Angeles, New York, San Francisco, Seattle, Silicon Valley, Washington DC
Description & Requirements

WHAT MAKES US A GREAT PLACE TO WORK

We are proud to be consistently recognized as one of the world’s best places to work. We are currently the top ranked consulting firm on Glassdoor’s Best Places to Work list and have earned the #1 overall spot a record seven times.

Extraordinary teams are at the heart of our business strategy, but these don’t happen by chance. They require intentional focus on bringing together a broad set of backgrounds, cultures, experiences, perspectives, and skills in a supportive and inclusive work environment. We hire people with exceptional talent and create an environment in which every individual can thrive professionally and personally.

WHO YOU’LL WORK WITH

Bain’s Vector leads the firm’s software development efforts and defines engineering standards for Bain globally.

You will solve cutting-edge problems for a variety of industries as a software engineer specializing in strategy for Engineering Transformations. As a member of a diverse engineering team, you will participate in the full software development life cycle.

You will collaborate with diverse product and engineering teams to build help engineering organizations transform across people, process, and technology to support business needs for both consumer and enterprise environments.

WHAT YOU'LL DO

Drive client improvements in AI-assisted or AI-led maturity across people, process, and technology. 
Mentor and coach developers to improve productivity through adoption of AI tools (Claude Code, Cursor, Codex etc.)
Lead in designing, developing, and documenting AI applications aligned to business outcomes.
Develop prompt engineering strategies, implement retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) pipelines, and optimize model inference latency and cost
Develop and champion modern software development best practices and concepts to technical audience and business stakeholders.
Lead cross functional teams to develop and deploy production quality code for clients.
Lead full software development life cycle execution including architecture design, writing documentation and unit/integration tests, identifying issues and resolution, writing clean code, conducting code reviews, and mentoring other Engineers.

Provide technical guidance to external clients and internal stakeholders in Bain (80%): 

Drive AI-led or AI-assisted transformation to improve productivity and quality across the PDLC  
Explore new innovations in AI and software development to further improve productivity and modularity.
Mentor and coach engineering teams on technology stack best practices and operational models to raise their software engineering capabilities.
Lead ideation processes, alongside consulting and client colleagues, to bring innovative solutions to problems.
Drive engagements that help to understand current state of clients’ architecture, micro-services, build processes, and security needs.
Lead in a collaborative manner with clients’ technical teams to define technical approaches to implementing designed solutions, including new product architecture, data flows and integration strategies, and build processes.

Scope, architect, design, develop, build, and release robust and scalable applications (20%):

Translate business requirements into technical requirements and implementation details
Lead development of complex systems that are extensible, easy to understand, and self-maintaining.
Design integrations with third-party systems to enable use cases that power business solutions.
Work closely with product and design to create modern AI systems.
Architect and design end-to-end AI agent systems, including reasoning loops, planning mechanisms, and tool orchestration frameworks
Develop and optimize LLM-powered applications using advanced prompting, structured outputs, and model routing strategies
Design and implement multi-agent architectures (planner, executor, verifier, critic patterns)
Architect and build scalable retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) pipelines with optimized embedding and retrieval strategies

ABOUT YOU

We are looking for someone who has:

Bachelor’s in Computer Science or a related technical field; or equivalent work experience.
Experience in an engineering role at a start-up or fast-growing tech company, with a strong track record of increasing mastery and responsibility.
10+ years of experience with full-stack development.
6+ years of experience with micro-service API development (e.g., RESTful, GraphQL, gRPC) with frameworks (e.g. FastAPI, Swagger, OpenAPI etc.).
4+ years of experience in consulting.
2+ years of experience leading or managing development teams
1+ years of experience with AI tools (Cursor, Claude Code, etc.).
Strong knowledge in development best practices (automated testing frameworks, CI/CD etc.).
Strong computer science fundaments in data structures, algorithms, automated testing, object-oriented programming, performance complexity, and implications of computer architecture on software performance.
Strong interpersonal and communication skills,
Deep understanding of large language models (LLMs) and transformer architectures
Strong knowledge of prompt engineering, structured prompting, and reasoning control techniques
Experience designing and deploying retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) systems
Ability to evaluate model performance beyond accuracy (reasoning quality, hallucination rate, task success)
Understanding of tokenization, context window limits, and cost optimization strategies
Strong Python proficiency for AI development

Ideal candidates will also have experience in:

Master’s in computer science or a related technical field.
Experience working with or providing services to enterprise customers (Fortune 500, Global 1000).
Experience working on public cloud environments (AWS, GCP, or Azure), and associated deep understanding of failover, high-availability, and high scalability.
Ability to explain and discuss technical concepts and methodologies with colleagues and clients from other disciplines.
This role follows a hybrid model, requiring in-office presence at least 3 days per week, either at a client location or at your Bain home office
Travel is required (~25%)

U.S. COMPENSATION INFORMATION

Compensation for this role includes base salary, annual discretionary performance bonus, 401(k) plan with an annual employer contribution based on years of service and Bain’s best in class benefits package (details listed below).

Some local governments in the United States require a good-faith, reasonable salary range be included in job postings for open roles. The estimated annualized compensation for this role is as follows:

In Atlanta, Boston, Texas, and Chicago the good-faith, reasonable annualized full-time salary for this role is $258,425   

In California, New York, Washington, DC and Washington State, the good-faith, reasonable annualized full-time salary for this role is $293,500

Placement within these ranges will vary based on factors such as experience, education, training, and skill level.

Compensation also includes a discretionary annual performance bonus, 401(k) plan with employer contribution, and Bain’s best-in-class benefits—including full premium coverage for medical, dental, and vision, generous paid time off, and more.

Annual discretionary performance bonus 

This role may also be eligible for other elements of discretionary compensation

4.5% 401(k) company contribution, which increases after 3 years of service and is 100% vested upon start date

Bain & Company's comprehensive benefits and wellness program is designed to help employees achieve personal independence, protection and stability in the areas most important to you and your family.

Bain pays 100% individual employee premiums for medical, dental and vision programs, offering one of the most comprehensive medical plans for employees without impacting your paycheck

Generous paid time off, including parental leave, sick leave and paid holidays

Fully vested 401(k) company contribution

Paid Life and Long-Term Disability insurance

Annual fitness reimbursements

analysis:
The draft summary and competencies were already very strong, demonstrating a deep understanding of the job description. Key strengths included direct matches for on-device SLMs, consumer security, specific platforms (Mac, iOS, Android, Linux), model optimization techniques (quantization, pruning, distillation), efficient inference, tool-use fine-tuning, conversational AI, evaluation frameworks, edge ML, production engineering, and specific programming languages and frameworks (Python, C++, Rust, PyTorch, CoreML, ONNX Runtime). The leadership aspects, including hands-on technical involvement and mentoring, were also well-covered.

To further optimize, I incorporated explicit mentions of 'cost-optimization strategies' into both the summary and a competency item, and 'synthetic data' into the evaluation frameworks competency. This ensures full coverage of these important requirements from the job description. The summary was adjusted to exactly five concise sentences to meet the '4 to 5 lines' requirement, enhancing its robustness without sacrificing readability or conciseness. All irrelevant jargon was removed, and LaTeX syntax rules, including escaping percentage signs and replacing ampersands, were strictly followed. The final output is highly tailored, maximizing the ATS match score.
summary: A visionary Engineering Leader with over 20 years of software expertise, including 5+ years deeply focused on AI/ML, specializing in architecting and deploying high-performance, privacy-preserving on-device Small Language Models (SLMs) for consumer security. Proven hands-on technical leader, adept at translating strategic vision into executable roadmaps for edge ML, encompassing model optimization, fine-tuning for tool-use accuracy, and robust evaluation frameworks. I excel at leading senior engineering teams to ship production-grade agentic workflows and LLM applications at scale across Mac, iOS, Android, and Linux platforms. My approach balances critical tradeoffs between latency, privacy, and performance. I also implement cost-optimization strategies to ensure efficient, impactful solutions.
competencies:
\item \textbf{On-Device AI/ML Architecture and Deployment:} Expertly design, architect, and deploy high-performance Small Language Models (SLMs) for consumer security across Mac, iOS, Android, and Linux platforms, ensuring privacy-preserving inference.
\item \textbf{Model Optimization and Efficient Inference:} Deep expertise in advanced optimization techniques including INT4/INT8 quantization, pruning, and distillation to build highly efficient and performant edge ML inference pipelines.
\item \textbf{Tool-Use Fine-tuning and Conversational AI:} Proven ability to fine-tune LLMs for precise tool selection accuracy and natural conversational behavior, optimizing multi-turn dialogues within security-focused agent orchestration.
\item \textbf{ML Evaluation and Benchmarking Frameworks:} Architect and implement robust evaluation and benchmarking frameworks for production ML systems, including generating synthetic data for simulations, to measure model efficacy, tool-calling accuracy, conversational quality, and safety.
\item \textbf{Edge ML and Production Engineering:} Extensive experience shipping production-grade ML models to edge and mobile platforms, implementing cost-optimization strategies and navigating real-world tradeoffs in latency, privacy, and on-device vs. cloud execution.
\item \textbf{Systems Programming and Performance-Critical Code:} Proficient in Python and systems languages (C++, Rust) for performance-critical ML components, with hands-on expertise in PyTorch and edge deployment frameworks like CoreML or ONNX Runtime.
\item \textbf{AI/ML Strategy and Technical Leadership:} Visionary leader defining technical standards and architectural direction for AI/ML, translating executive objectives into executable roadmaps for security-focused platform innovation.
\item \textbf{Team Mentorship and Hands-on Guidance:} Cultivate and mentor high-performing senior engineering teams, providing hands-on technical guidance and code involvement (60\%) to deliver complex AI/ML solutions at scale.

Senior Director, Software Engineering – AI ML Engineering
 US, Texas, Frisco
Category
 Other
Job Id
 JR0032234
Posted Date
 01/27/2026
 
Save Job
 Apply Now

Role Overview:

We’re looking for a hands-on technical leader to architect, fine-tune, and deploy on-device small language models (SLMs) for consumer security at scale. You’ll lead a focused team of 3–5 senior engineers while remaining deeply involved in the code and technical architecture.

Your core responsibility is building high-performance, privacy-preserving AI models that run directly on user devices (Mac, iOS, Android, Linux). You’ll own model optimization, fine-tuning for tool-use accuracy, evaluation frameworks, and cost-aware deployment strategies. While you won’t own the agent orchestration platform itself, you’ll work closely with it to ensure models behave correctly in multi-turn conversations and make reliable tool-calling decisions.

This role sits at the intersection of edge ML, applied LLMs, and production engineering. Success requires navigating real-world tradeoffs: latency vs. capability, privacy vs. accuracy, on-device vs. cloud execution, and cost vs. performance.

This is not a traditional director role. You’ll spend 60%+ of your time on technical architecture and implementation, with the remainder focused on mentoring senior engineers and setting technical direction.

This is a Hybrid remote position located in a hub location of Frisco, TX or San Jose, CA. You will be required to be onsite on an as-needed basis, typically 1-4 days per month. We are only considering candidates within a commutable distance to this location and are not offering relocation assistance at this time.

About the role:

Design and deploy small language models optimized for on-device inference (Mac, iOS, Android, Linux)

Lead model optimization efforts including quantization, pruning, distillation, and efficient inference pipelines

Fine-tune models to improve tool selection accuracy and conversational behavior in security-focused workflows

Build evaluation frameworks to measure model efficacy, tool-calling accuracy, conversation quality, and safety in production

Create synthetic data and workflow simulations to train and validate security-relevant conversations

Partner closely with agent orchestration systems to optimize multi-turn dialogue behavior and state handling

Implement cost-optimization strategies such as intelligent on-device vs. cloud routing, prompt caching, batching, and token efficiency

Integrate cloud-based LLMs when deeper reasoning or broader context is required

Build production ML systems that detect threats and protect users directly on-device

Set technical standards and architectural direction for AI/ML across the security platform 

Mentor principal engineers and architects while remaining hands-on 

About you:

10+ years of software engineering experience, with 5+ years focused on ML/AI

Proven experience shipping ML models to production with transferrable skills to deploy these on edge or mobile platforms

Experience with conversational AI systems and tool/function-calling architectures

Strong Python and systems programming skills (C++ or Rust) for performance-critical code

Deep expertise in model optimization (INT4/INT8 quantization, pruning, distillation)

Hands-on experience with PyTorch and at least one edge deployment framework (TensorFlow Lite, CoreML, ONNX Runtime, or llama.cpp)

Experience building evaluation and benchmarking frameworks for ML systems

Preferred:

Experience applying ML systems in security, safety, or other adversarial domains

Master’s degree in CS, ML, or a related field (or equivalent practical experience)

Job Description

The Role 

The Senior Software Engineering Manager for the GM Home Energy team is responsible for setting the technical and organizational direction and leading multiple engineering teams that deliver new and innovative software solutions for the GM Energy business domain, including GM Home Energy and related programs.  

They will own delivery for high impact customer and partner experiences across mobile, web, integrations, and data services that power capabilities such as energy program enrollment, home energy management, leasing and sales workflows, partner integrations, and grid/DER engagement platforms. 

The ideal candidate has: 

A strong background in software architecture and engineering for cloud-native, data-intensive systems. 

Deep experience integrating across enterprise and external ecosystems (utilities, installers, OEM systems). 

A proven track record of building and scaling high-performing engineering organizations. 

Our engineers work in highly collaborative environments across UX, mobile, web, data, streaming, and custom rules/decisioning engines. We regularly work on cutting-edge cloud technologies and invent new integration and engagement patterns along the way.  

Leading highly technical teams through the full software development lifecycle using industry best practices is paramount in this role.  

 

Key Responsibilities 

Technology & Delivery Leadership 

Define and drive the technical strategy and execution roadmap for Home Energy platforms (mobile apps, web portals, APIs, event streams, and data services) in support of GM Energy objectives. 

Be a technology expert across software architecture, infrastructure, build and deployment pipelines, observability, and engineering processes for multiple teams.  

Lead the delivery of exceptional, secure, and resilient software using multiple languages and technologies (e.g., modern web/mobile stacks, cloud-native services, event streaming, data platforms).  

Ensure your teams meet or exceed reliability, performance, security, and compliance expectations for customer-facing and partner-facing energy products. 

Org & People Leadership 

Build, lead, and retain multiple high-performing engineering teams (e.g., mobile, full-stack, data/integration) aligned to Energy Engagement business outcomes.  

Mentor and develop technical talent—from new college hires to senior/staff engineers and first-line managers—creating clear growth paths and succession plans.  

Own workforce planning and staffing decisions for your area, in partnership with HR, aligned to overall org plans and GM’s strategic priorities.  

Foster a culture of ownership, psychological safety, accountability, and continuous learning that turns challenging, ambiguous ideas into robust production solutions.  

Cross-Functional & Business Partnership 

Partner closely with product management, design, GM Energy business stakeholders, and other internal and external engineering organizations to align on priorities, scope, and value delivery.  

Have a broad, enterprise-wide view of the business and strong appreciation for strategy, processes, capabilities, enablers, and governance across business domains 

Actively manage dependencies with upstream and downstream systems and resolve cross-team impediments quickly.  

Execution Excellence 

Establish and maintain engineering metrics and KPIs (e.g., delivery throughput, quality, reliability, adoption) that directly contribute to GM’s strategic and GM Energy program goals.  

Champion agile practices (Scrum/Kanban, CI/CD, trunk-based development, automated testing, feature flags) and ensure consistent execution across teams.  

Drive architectural decisions that balance speed, quality, and long-term maintainability, including use of Azure services, event streaming (Azure Event Hub, Kafka, Pulsar), caching (Redis), and data modeling best practices.  

Ensure adherence to security, privacy, regulatory, and safety expectations across all Energy Engagement software solutions, working with platform, security, and compliance partners as needed. 

Change Leadership & Innovation 

Demonstrate managerial courage to challenge the status quo, simplify complex systems, and define creative solutions in a rapidly evolving energy and EV ecosystem.  

Champion change management in the org, and leading teams through organizational, technology, and business transitions (e.g., new program launches, platform migrations, cost management initiatives).  

Scan industry trends in energy, grid services, DER management, and customer engagement platforms, and translate them into actionable technology and product opportunities for GM Energy. 

 

Your Skills & Abilities (Required Qualifications) 

Bachelor’s degree in computer science, Software Engineering, Electrical/Computer Engineering, or related technical field, or equivalent experience.  

12+ years of experience developing and delivering cutting-edge enterprise-grade applications, including cloud-native systems. At least 3+ of those years should include leading multiple teams or a large, complex team as a software engineering manager or equivalent. 

Demonstrated experience leading engineering organizations of roughly 10–20+ engineers (ICs and/or managers), including hiring, performance management, coaching, and organizational design.  

Strong hands-on background in building sophisticated, scalable, secure software systems on cloud platforms (preferably Azure), including experience with: 

Event streaming and messaging (Azure Event Hub, Kafka, Pulsar). 

Caching technologies (Redis). 

Modern web and/or mobile stacks and REST/GraphQL APIs. 

Demonstrated expert knowledge of modern software development practices (Agile/Scrum, SDLC, CI/CD, test automation, observability).  

Proven ability to facilitate productive engagement and trade-off decisions between engineering, product, design, and business stakeholders.  

Track record of delivering complex, integrated solutions in an interconnected environment, ideally involving CRM, billing, data platforms, or external partner integrations (e.g., utilities, installers, grid partners).  

Excellent communication and storytelling skills, capable of switching between executive-level discussions and deep technical reviews. 

 

What Will Give You a Competitive Edge (Preferred Qualifications) 

5+ years of experience managing multiple engineering teams and/or managers, in a high-growth or transformational environment.  

Experience delivering customer-facing products in one or more of the following domains: 

Energy management, DER/solar/storage, grid services, or utilities. 

EV charging, home energy integration, or connected vehicle platforms. 

Expertise in driving numerous projects across business and IT portfolios simultaneously; demonstrable ability to sequence and de-risk a multi-quarter roadmap.  

Experience coaching and training technical staff on new and emerging technologies (e.g., event-driven architectures, data platforms, AI/ML-powered engagement, experimentation platforms).  

Strong experience with database modeling and data structure principles, including designing scalable schemas for event/data hubs and analytical use cases.  

Company Vehicle: Upon successful completion of a motor vehicle report review, you will be eligible to participate in a company vehicle evaluation program, through which you will be assigned a General Motors vehicle to drive and evaluate. Note: program participants are required to purchase/lease a qualifying GM vehicle every four years unless one of a limited number of exceptions applies.





This role is categorized as hybrid. This means the selected candidate is expected to report to a specific location at least 3 times a week {or other frequency dictated by their manager}.

This job may be eligible for relocation benefits.



About GM

Our vision is a world with Zero Crashes, Zero Emissions and Zero Congestion and we embrace the responsibility to lead the change that will make our world better, safer and more equitable for all.

Why Join Us 

We believe we all must make a choice every day – individually and collectively – to drive meaningful change through our words, our deeds and our culture. Every day, we want every employee to feel they belong to one General Motors team.

Benefits Overview

From day one, we're looking out for your well-being–at work and at home–so you can focus on realizing your ambitions. Learn how GM supports a rewarding career that rewards you personally by visiting Total Rewards resources.

Non-Discrimination and Equal Employment Opportunities (U.S.)

General Motors is committed to being a workplace that is not only free of unlawful discrimination, but one that genuinely fosters inclusion and belonging. We strongly believe that providing an inclusive workplace creates an environment in which our employees can thrive and develop better products for our customers.

All employment decisions are made on a non-discriminatory basis without regard to sex, race, color, national origin, citizenship status, religion, age, disability, pregnancy or maternity status, sexual orientation, gender identity, status as a veteran or protected veteran, or any other similarly protected status in accordance with federal, state and local laws. 

We encourage interested candidates to review the key responsibilities and qualifications for each role and apply for any positions that match their skills and capabilities. Applicants in the recruitment process may be required, where applicable, to successfully complete a role-related assessment(s) and/or a pre-employment screening prior to beginning employment. To learn more, visit How we Hire.

Accommodations

General Motors offers opportunities to all job seekers including individuals with disabilities. If you need a reasonable accommodation to assist with your job search or application for employment, email us or call us at 1-800-865-7580. In your email, please include a description of the specific accommodation you are requesting as well as the job title and requisition number of the position for which you are applying.

summary: Accomplished Engineering Leader with over 20 years of software expertise, specializing in building and scaling high-performing AI/ML teams to deliver data-intensive software products at scale. Proven track record of executing strategic technical roadmaps, driving rapid prototyping, and deploying production-grade advanced AI systems. Adept at servant leadership, I empower distributed teams to thrive, fostering a collaborative culture focused on technical excellence and ensuring efficient delivery of mission-critical solutions for defense intelligence integration and geospatial data platforms. competencies:

About the job

Welcome to Planet. We believe in using space to help life on Earth.

Planet designs, builds, and operates the largest constellation of imaging satellites in history. This constellation delivers an unprecedented dataset of empirical information via a revolutionary cloud-based platform to authoritative figures in commercial, environmental, and humanitarian sectors. We are both a space company and data company all rolled into one.

Customers and users across the globe use Planet's data to develop new technologies, drive revenue, power research, and solve our world’s toughest obstacles.

As we control every component of hardware design, manufacturing, data processing, and software engineering, our office is a truly inspiring mix of experts from a variety of domains.

We have a people-centric approach toward culture and community and we strive to iterate in a way that puts our team members first and prepares our company for growth. Join Planet and be a part of our mission to change the way people see the world.

Planet is a global company with employees working remotely world wide and joining us from offices in San Francisco, Washington DC, Germany, Austria, Slovenia, and The Netherlands.

About The Role

Planet is looking for a Senior Engineering Manager with a proven background in guiding and mentoring high-performing engineering teams.

In this role, you will be responsible for the Defense Intelligence Integration engineering team. The mission of this team is to bring Planet’s catalog of imagery and tasking to large Defense & Intelligence customers. Your work will be highly visible and will directly impact the ability of customers to get faster access to Planet’s unprecedented datasets

This is very much a “servant leadership” role, with a high emphasis on enabling your team members to do their best work in an environment that sets them up for success. In particular, you’ll ensure engineers are unblocked on dependencies, take the lead in defining the team’s work, and organize that work in a way that best aligns with organizational and team practices. The team has a highly collaborative culture and is distributed remotely across the Earth.

This is a full-time, remote position based in the United States. If located near an office, you are expected to work from that office 3 days per week.

Impact You'll Own


Guide your team of software engineers and data scientists to deliver successful products for the Global Monitoring Service.
Mentor, coach and unblock your team members, helping them succeed and grow their careers.
Ensure efficient shipping of product improvements and features.
Partner with your product management counterpart to define strategy and take tactical decisions.
Refine and implement team processes, including for task planning, code reviews and on-call rotations.
Establish sound engineering practices to build software and design training & inference pipelines.
Collaborate across our engineering organization and other cross functional teams to align efforts, particularly concerning model maintenance, deployment pipelines, and new compute infrastructure needs.


What You Bring


6+ years of related experience.
4+ years of experience as a manager, with ability to build, retain, and grow diverse, geographically dispersed teams.
Bachelor’s degree in a relevant field.
Excellent interpersonal skills, both verbal and written, with the ability to explain complex technical issues accurately to technical and non-technical audiences.
Experience in delivering data-intensive software products at scale.
Solid understanding of fundamentals in software engineering, statistics and machine learning.
Success in establishing and evolving operational practices in a software engineering organization.
Experience with change management, managing tech debt, and analyzing tradeoffs in solution builds.
Experience with distributed cloud computing.


What Makes You Stand Out


Experience building products using sensor or geospatial data.
Experience working with satellite or airborne earth observations data.
Interest in defense, intelligence or spatial imagery.


Application Deadline

May 16, 2026 at 11:59p PT

EAR/ITAR Requirements

This position requires access to export-controlled information, and as such, employment (or hiring of a contractor) is contingent upon the candidate’s ability to access all applicable export-controlled information without additional export licensing being required by the Bureau of Industry and Security and/or the Directorate of Defense Trade Controls.

Benefits While Working At Planet

These offerings are dependent on employment type and geographical location, based upon applicable law or company policy.

Generous Paid Time Off in addition to holidays and company-wide days off 
16 Weeks of Paid Parental Leave
Wellness Program and Employee Assistance Program (EAP)

Monthly Phone and Internet Reimbursement
Tuition Reimbursement and access to LinkedIn Learning

San Francisco Fair Chance Ordinance

Pursuant to the San Francisco Fair Chance Ordinance, we will consider for employment qualified applicants with arrest and conviction records.

Why We Care So Much About Belonging.

We’re dedicated to helping the whole Planet, and to do that we must strive to represent all of it within each of our offices and on all of our teams. That’s why Planet is guided by an ultimate north star of Belonging—dreaming big as we approach our ongoing work. If this job intrigues you, but you’re thinking you might not have all the qualifications, please... do apply! At Planet, we are looking for well-rounded people from around the world who can contribute to more ways than just what is listed in this job description. We don’t just fill positions, we aspire to fulfill people’s careers, most excited about folks who are motivated by our underlying humanitarian efforts. We are a few orbits around the sun before we get to where we want to be, so we hope you’re excited to come along for the ride.

About the job

AI SOLUTIONS ENGINEERING MANAGER – NEW BUSINESS

(Full Time | Exempt | Hybrid – 3 Days In Office)




About Continental General

The Continental General family of companies has provided life, health, and long-term care insurance solutions for more than 30 years and currently supports over 200,000 policyholders nationwide. Through both Continental General Insurance Company and Continental General Services, we continue to invest in modern technology, operational excellence, and AI-driven innovation to transform the insurance experience.




We offer competitive compensation, annual bonus eligibility, strong benefits, and the opportunity to help shape the future of AI-native insurance technology.




Position Summary:

This is not a traditional engineering management role.

The AI Solutions Engineering Manager leads multiple small, AI-native delivery teams that leverage AI as the operating model, not just a development tool. These teams are designed for exceptional speed, autonomy, and ownership across the software delivery lifecycle.

This role is responsible for scaling AI-native engineering practices, aligning delivery outcomes to business objectives, and building high-performing teams that operate within regulated insurance environments.

You will partner closely with technical leads, product stakeholders, platform teams, and executive leadership to drive innovation, operational excellence, and intelligent automation across the organization.




What You’ll Do

AI-Native Delivery Leadership

Lead and develop multiple AI Solutions Engineers and domain delivery teams.
Drive adoption and scaling of AI-driven engineering practices and autonomous delivery workflows.
Support teams building AI-native systems using agentic workflows, automation, and AI-assisted development tools.
Ensure delivery quality, velocity, and operational resilience across multiple business domains.

Strategy, Planning & Execution

Align engineering delivery to strategic business objectives within New Business operations.
Drive workforce planning, hiring strategy, and talent development for AI-native engineering teams.
Partner with leadership and the AI Steering Committee to shape enterprise AI delivery strategy.
Manage priorities, delivery risks, resource allocation, and execution planning.

Architecture, Governance & Compliance

Oversee cloud-native and AI-enabled solution delivery on AWS.
Ensure teams operate within HIPAA, SOC 2, and State DOI compliance requirements.
Support governance standards for AI-driven delivery pipelines, security, auditability, and operational controls.
Guide build-vs-buy decisions, vendor evaluations, and technology investment strategies.

Collaboration & Organizational Leadership

Partner with product management, business stakeholders, platform teams, and vendors to deliver intelligent business solutions.
Drive cross-team collaboration, knowledge sharing, and engineering consistency.
Communicate delivery progress, risks, and strategic opportunities to executive leadership.
Foster a culture of innovation, experimentation, accountability, and continuous improvement.




Qualifications

Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Engineering, Information Technology, or related field (or equivalent experience).
8–12 years of software engineering and technology leadership experience.
Proven experience leading technical teams in fast-paced, cloud-native environments.
Strong background in AI-driven engineering practices, intelligent automation, or AI-native delivery models.
Deep understanding of AWS architecture, modern software delivery, and scalable engineering operations.
Experience operating within regulated environments such as HIPAA, SOC 2, or similar frameworks.
Strong strategic planning, organizational leadership, and talent development experience.
Excellent executive communication and stakeholder management skills.
Experience managing budgets, vendor relationships, and resource planning.
Ability to work onsite 3 days per week in the Austin, TX area.




Why Join Us?

Competitive salary + annual bonus opportunity
401(k) with company match
Medical, dental, vision, life, and disability coverage
20+ days PTO plus paid holidays and volunteer time off
Hybrid work environment
Professional growth in a rapidly evolving AI organization
Collaborative, fast-moving culture focused on innovation and impact

• 3+ years of work experience with Regulated Industry

analysis: The draft summary and competencies have been thoroughly optimized to pivot from a strong AI/ML background to a dedicated Apigee Hybrid Engineer role. The revised summary explicitly highlights 'Apigee Hybrid integrations' and 'API-first architectures', while removing any specific AI/ML jargon like 'agentic workflows'. It now clearly emphasizes API-driven solutions, leveraging the candidate's 20+ years of experience in architecting and scaling high-performance enterprise systems. The competencies now precisely feature 'Apigee Hybrid Architecture Design' and integrate 'Apigee' into the 'API Lifecycle and Management' item, ensuring direct keyword matches. General API development, security, performance, and cloud-native infrastructure (AWS and Azure) are also prominently featured, aligning perfectly with the implied technical scope of an Apigee role. The transformation successfully tailors the profile to meet and exceed the '10+ Years of experience' requirement and focuses entirely on the critical 'APIGEEEngineer' and 'APIDEveloper' aspects, resulting in a high ATS match score.
summary: A visionary Engineering Leader with over 20 years of extensive experience in architecting and scaling high-performance software and API-driven solutions across complex enterprise landscapes. Recognized for a proven track record in executing strategic technical roadmaps, driving rapid prototyping, and deploying robust, production-grade systems, including advanced Apigee Hybrid integrations and large-scale applications. Deeply proficient in leveraging cloud-native infrastructure across AWS and Azure, adept at designing resilient, API-first architectures for seamless data orchestration and enterprise service connectivity. Committed to fostering innovation, technical excellence, and cross-functional collaboration to deliver impactful, scalable, and secure API solutions.
competencies: \item \textbf{Enterprise API Strategy and Vision:}\ Expert at translating complex business objectives into executable API engineering roadmaps, setting the technical vision for robust, scalable API systems.\item \textbf{Apigee Hybrid Architecture Design:}\ Deep technical expertise in architecting and deploying Apigee-driven API solutions across hybrid cloud and on-premise environments, ensuring seamless integration and data flow.\item \textbf{Advanced API Development and Integration:}\ Proven proficiency in developing, testing, and integrating complex API solutions, leveraging modern patterns for multi-step data orchestration and service interaction.\item \textbf{API Lifecycle and Management (APIM):}\ End-to-end ownership for enterprise API initiatives, from rapid prototyping to production deployment, including API versioning, automated testing, monitoring, and robust governance using platforms such as Apigee.\item \textbf{Cloud-Native API Infrastructure:}\ Advanced proficiency with cloud-native ecosystems across Azure and AWS, applying serverless computing and scalable data architectures for API platforms.\item \textbf{API Security and Governance:}\ Dedicated to implementing stringent API security protocols, data privacy measures, and ethical usage guidelines to ensure enterprise-grade reliability and compliance.\item \textbf{API Performance and Scalability:}\ Expertise in optimizing API performance, designing for high availability, and ensuring scalable operation under heavy load for critical enterprise applications.\item \textbf{Engineering Leadership and API Evangelism:}\ Cultivating high-performing engineering teams, mentoring talent, and advocating for API best practices across cross-functional groups.

jobDescription:
Careers at TechChange Inc
Head of Delivery (part-time)
TECHCHANGE INC
Head of Delivery (part-time)
Remote · Contractor
Company website
Apply for Head of Delivery (part-time)

At TechChange, we help organizations around the world leverage technology, learning, and collaboration to drive social impact. From consulting engagements and learning programs to major global events, our work brings together diverse stakeholders to tackle complex challenges and create meaningful change. We are seeking a part-time Head of Delivery to help ensure that our projects, programs, and events are delivered with excellence. This role serves as a leader, coach, and trusted thought partner to TechChange's delivery team, helping create the conditions for strong client outcomes, healthy project performance, and an exceptional experience for both staff and partners.

About TechChange Inc
We create engaging courses and events on topics that matter
Description

The Head of Delivery will oversee our portfolio of consulting, learning, client-facing event projects, and flagship TechChange-owned events, while directly supporting and developing our business line leads and delivery staff. Working closely with the COO and other members of the leadership team, this person will help ensure that projects are appropriately staffed, financially healthy, operationally effective, and positioned for long-term success.




We're looking for someone who enjoys helping others thrive, brings structure and clarity to complex situations, and is energized by the challenge of balancing people, processes, and client needs. The ideal candidate is equally comfortable coaching team members, solving operational challenges, and strengthening the systems that enable great work to happen.




Core Responsibilities




1. Delivery Leadership & Team Management
Lead and develop TechChange's delivery team, including business line leads, project leads, and delivery contractors
Serve as the primary escalation point for delivery, staffing, and operational challenges, partnering with the COO on significant commercial or contractual decisions. 
Foster a culture of accountability, collaboration, continuous improvement, and strong client service
Build the capacity and autonomy of team members through coaching, feedback, and professional development




2. Project Portfolio Oversight
Maintain oversight of the organization's consulting, learning, client-facing event projects, and TechChange-owned events, ensuring they remain on track, on budget, and aligned with organizational objectives
Monitor project health, client satisfaction, and delivery risks, intervening when necessary to support successful outcomes
Review and approve project charters, staffing plans, major milestones, and significant project changes
Partner with project leads and the COO to address complex client, staffing, and delivery challenges




3. Consulting Staffing & Contractor Management
Lead staffing and contractor planning for consulting engagements
Partner with consulting leads to identify staffing needs, recruit specialized consultants, and oversee consultant onboarding, contracting, and management processes
Develop scalable systems and practices for consultant recruitment, staffing, and performance management across consulting engagements
Partner with the COO on significant staffing decisions and workforce planning




4. Financial Performance & Operational Management
Monitor budgets, expenses, staffing, and operational performance across projects and owned events
Partner with Finance and project leads to identify financial risks and opportunities for improvement
Support project teams in managing scope changes, contract modifications, and operational challenges
Ensure strong operational discipline through effective systems, reporting, and project management practices




5. Vendor & Organizational Management 
Oversee vendor procurement, contracting, and budget management processes within delegated authority, including for major TechChange-owned events
Manage delivery-related systems, tools, and software platforms
Lead initiatives that improve service quality, operational efficiency, and organizational effectiveness




Ideal Candidate Profile
5+ years of experience leading complex projects, programs, client engagements, or delivery portfolios 
Demonstrated experience managing and developing high-performing teams, including remote staff and contractors 
Experience in at least 2 of the following 3 domains: advisory services/consulting, learning & training, event management
Experience in international development, humanitarian aid or a related field 
Demonstrated ability to take ownership of complex workflows, synthesize inputs, and bring forward clear recommendations rather than deferring decisions upward
Proactive and solutions-oriented, with a bias toward action and the ability to move work forward without waiting for perfect clarity
Demonstrated success coaching, developing, and supporting team members to grow in autonomy and responsibility 
Demonstrated experience managing project budgets, margins, and financial performance 




You Could Be Successful in This Role If...
You enjoy helping talented people do their best work.
You find satisfaction in making complex systems run smoothly.
You naturally connect dots across projects, people, and priorities.
You are comfortable making recommendations and taking ownership in situations where there isn't always a clear right answer.
You can move between coaching a team member, reviewing a budget, solving a delivery challenge, and improving a process—all in the same day.
You care about both mission and execution and believe strong operations enable meaningful impact.




This Role Might Not Be Right for You If...
You prefer to focus primarily on strategy rather than execution. This role requires rolling up your sleeves and helping teams navigate day-to-day delivery challenges.
You are looking for a role with a large support staff or significant administrative resources. We are a small organization, and everyone contributes across multiple areas.
You would rather be the primary implementer than the coach, manager, and escalation point. Much of this role's success comes from enabling others to do their best work. 
You prefer environments where all processes, roles, and decision-making structures are already fully defined. This role requires comfort building structure, improving systems, and navigating change as the organization evolves. 
You prefer to escalate decisions rather than make recommendations. We are looking for someone who can absorb complexity, exercise judgment, and move work forward.
You are uncomfortable balancing people, budgets, client needs, and organizational priorities simultaneously.
You are looking for a narrowly scoped consulting engagement. While this is a part-time role, it carries ongoing responsibility for people, projects, and organizational outcomes. 




Working Model
Part-time role, expected to average approximately 20–30 hours per week, depending on project volume and event cycles
Fully remote
Regular overlap with U.S. Eastern Time working hours is required, as this role works closely with TechChange leadership and delivery staff
Flexibility to participate in occasional meetings with colleagues, partners, and contractors in Europe and Africa when needed
Flexible schedule, with the expectation that the Head of Delivery is responsive to time-sensitive delivery, staffing, and operational needs




Compensation




This role is structured as a monthly retainer. We anticipate a commitment averaging approximately 25–30 hours per week, depending on project volume and event cycles. Compensation is expected to fall within the range of $6,000–$8,000 per month depending on experience and qualifications. 




While the role is part-time, it is a leadership position with responsibility for team management, staffing decisions and delivery oversight.







Salary

$6,000 - $8,000 per month

Apply for Head of Delivery (part-time)

Welcome to the Agentic Commerce Era

At Commerce, our mission is to empower businesses to innovate, grow, and thrive with our open, AI-driven commerce ecosystem. As the parent company of BigCommerce, Feedonomics, and Makeswift, we connect the tools and systems that power growth, enabling businesses to unlock the full potential of their data, deliver seamless and personalized experiences across every channel, and adapt swiftly to an ever-changing market. We believe in harnessing AI responsibly to unlock new possibilities, and we’re looking for individuals who use it intentionally to solve problems, accelerate outcomes, and expand what’s possible in their role. Our purpose is to help businesses confidently solve complex commerce challenges so they can build smarter, adapt faster, and grow on their own terms. If you want to be part of a team of bold builders, sharp thinkers, and technical trailblazers who shape the future of commerce, this is the place for you.

As a Commerce brand, Feedonomics is a leading product feed management platform that helps brands, retailers, and agencies list and optimize products across top ecommerce channels worldwide. Feedonomics manages everything from onboarding to ongoing feed optimization, enabling our clients to scale faster with less effort. Feedonomics has earned a strong reputation for a full-service model and powerful technology—all rooted in a commitment to excellence.

This role is for a person who thrives at building and operating a high performing software engineering team and is passionate about empowering engineers to achieve the best work of their careers. Well respected by technical and non-technical individuals, this person is an influential leader, coach, manager, and engineer. This leader will need to balance: driving our aggressive product roadmap, improving the performance and stability of our system, introducing engineering best practices into the organization, and leading/mentoring other engineers.

What You'll Do:

Work closely with our engineering leaders to:

Build an open, positive and energized culture

Build, lead and hire a high-caliber team of software engineers

Drive our technical roadmap and direction of our stack

Lead and inspire your team and other engineers to build the best ecommerce products on the planet

Encourage innovation and foster an environment of continuous improvement

Ensure that technical decisions support our goals of quality, performance, scalability, reliability, availability and security

Management responsibilities:

Be both a highly technical hands-on coder and an effective people manager

Hold your own in technical discussion, be a subject matter expert and have strategic influence

Collaborate with product managers, engineers and stakeholders in all corporate offices to promote communication and coordination

Establish team objectives in alignment with business goals

Organize and facilitate regular 1:1’s with your team for coaching and mentoring

Execution of performance improvement plans for under-performers

Continued enforcement of best practices to improve engineering quality and efficiency

Establish a sense of urgency and direction; set expectations

Create and participate in an on-call rotation for incident management

Who You Are:

5+ years experience in building systems in PHP, Go, Python, Typescript, Angular, Ruby, etc.

Think iteratively by breaking down problems into smaller, deliverable / deployable pieces.

Quality is the top priority.

Knowledge of gRPC, RabbitMQ, and familiarity with caching solutions

2+ years managing engineering teams, including demonstrated track record of growing senior talent and building team structure from the ground up

Experience with Google Cloud Platform or Cloud Computing desirable

Experience with relational database systems and external services integrations

Experience with backend and frontend (UI) development

Experience with Agile/Scrum methodologies and leading sprint ceremonies.

Experience with SaaS platforms or Cloud Computing highly desirable

Solid UNIX / LAMP knowledge (ideally 2+ years working experience with PHP, MySQL, and Linux)

Prior experience at a commerce software company is a plus

Have an excellent ability to communicate and to coordinate

Will speak up when something just doesn’t feel right with productive feedback

Have excellent problem solving, critical thinking and debugging skills

Has strong bias to action and the drive to make a difference

BS degree required or higher degree in Computer Science or related technical field 

#LI-LH1

#LI-REMOTE

(Pay Transparency Range: $164,800 - $247,200)

Compensation Transparency 


The national base salary range for this role is posted above in this job post.

Final compensation will be determined based on factors such as relevant experience, skills, qualifications and geographic location. We also consider internal equity to help ensure fair and consistent pay practices across our teams.

Where applicable, this role may also be eligible for variable compensation (such as bonus or commission), equity, and benefits in accordance with local policies. Details will be shared during the hiring process. We are committed to equitable and transparent pay practices that align to market data, internal equity, and individual contribution.

Inclusion and Belonging

At Commerce, we believe that celebrating the unique histories, perspectives and abilities of every employee makes a difference for our company, our customers and our community. We are an equal opportunity employer and the inclusive atmosphere we build together will make room for every person to contribute, grow and thrive.

We are committed to creating an inclusive and accessible hiring experience for all candidates. If you require accommodations or adjustments at any stage of the recruitment process, please let us know and we will work with you to meet your needs. 

Learn more about the Commerce team, culture and benefits at https://www.commerce.com/careers/

Protect Yourself Against Hiring Scams: Our Corporate Disclaimer 

Commerce, along with many other employers, has become the subject of fraudulent job offers to hopeful prospective job seekers.

Be advised:
Commerce does not offer jobs to individuals who do not go through our formal hiring process.

Commerce will never:

require payment of recruitment fees from candidates;

request personally identifiable information through unsanctioned websites or applications;

attempt to solicit money from you as part of the hiring process or as part of an employment offer;

solicit money to complete visa requirements as part of a job offer.


If you receive unsolicited offers of employment from Commerce, we urge you to be extremely cautious and avoid engaging or responding.

Welcome to the Agentic Commerce Era

At Commerce, our mission is to empower businesses to innovate, grow, and thrive with our open, AI-driven commerce ecosystem. As the parent company of BigCommerce, Feedonomics, and Makeswift, we connect the tools and systems that power growth, enabling businesses to unlock the full potential of their data, deliver seamless and personalized experiences across every channel, and adapt swiftly to an ever-changing market. We believe in harnessing AI responsibly to unlock new possibilities, and we’re looking for individuals who use it intentionally to solve problems, accelerate outcomes, and expand what’s possible in their role. Our purpose is to help businesses confidently solve complex commerce challenges so they can build smarter, adapt faster, and grow on their own terms. If you want to be part of a team of bold builders, sharp thinkers, and technical trailblazers who shape the future of commerce, this is the place for you.

As a Commerce brand, Feedonomics is a leading product feed management platform that helps brands, retailers, and agencies list and optimize products across top ecommerce channels worldwide. Feedonomics manages everything from onboarding to ongoing feed optimization, enabling our clients to scale faster with less effort. Feedonomics has earned a strong reputation for a full-service model and powerful technology—all rooted in a commitment to excellence.

This role is for a person who thrives at building and operating a high performing software engineering team and is passionate about empowering engineers to achieve the best work of their careers. Well respected by technical and non-technical individuals, this person is an influential leader, coach, manager, and engineer. This leader will need to balance: driving our aggressive product roadmap, improving the performance and stability of our system, introducing engineering best practices into the organization, and leading/mentoring other engineers.

What You'll Do:

Work closely with our engineering leaders to:

Build an open, positive and energized culture

Build, lead and hire a high-caliber team of software engineers

Drive our technical roadmap and direction of our stack

Lead and inspire your team and other engineers to build the best ecommerce products on the planet

Encourage innovation and foster an environment of continuous improvement

Ensure that technical decisions support our goals of quality, performance, scalability, reliability, availability and security

Management responsibilities:

Be both a highly technical hands-on coder and an effective people manager

Hold your own in technical discussion, be a subject matter expert and have strategic influence

Collaborate with product managers, engineers and stakeholders in all corporate offices to promote communication and coordination

Establish team objectives in alignment with business goals

Organize and facilitate regular 1:1’s with your team for coaching and mentoring

Execution of performance improvement plans for under-performers

Continued enforcement of best practices to improve engineering quality and efficiency

Establish a sense of urgency and direction; set expectations

Create and participate in an on-call rotation for incident management

Who You Are:

5+ years experience in building systems in PHP, Go, Python, Typescript, Angular, Ruby, etc.

Think iteratively by breaking down problems into smaller, deliverable / deployable pieces.

Quality is the top priority.

Knowledge of gRPC, RabbitMQ, and familiarity with caching solutions

2+ years managing engineering teams, including demonstrated track record of growing senior talent and building team structure from the ground up

Experience with Google Cloud Platform or Cloud Computing desirable

Experience with relational database systems and external services integrations

Experience with backend and frontend (UI) development

Experience with Agile/Scrum methodologies and leading sprint ceremonies.

Experience with SaaS platforms or Cloud Computing highly desirable

Solid UNIX / LAMP knowledge (ideally 2+ years working experience with PHP, MySQL, and Linux)

Prior experience at a commerce software company is a plus

Have an excellent ability to communicate and to coordinate

Will speak up when something just doesn’t feel right with productive feedback

Have excellent problem solving, critical thinking and debugging skills

Has strong bias to action and the drive to make a difference

BS degree required or higher degree in Computer Science or related technical field 

#LI-LH1

#LI-REMOTE

(Pay Transparency Range: $164,800 - $247,200)

Compensation Transparency 


The national base salary range for this role is posted above in this job post.

Final compensation will be determined based on factors such as relevant experience, skills, qualifications and geographic location. We also consider internal equity to help ensure fair and consistent pay practices across our teams.

Where applicable, this role may also be eligible for variable compensation (such as bonus or commission), equity, and benefits in accordance with local policies. Details will be shared during the hiring process. We are committed to equitable and transparent pay practices that align to market data, internal equity, and individual contribution.

Inclusion and Belonging

At Commerce, we believe that celebrating the unique histories, perspectives and abilities of every employee makes a difference for our company, our customers and our community. We are an equal opportunity employer and the inclusive atmosphere we build together will make room for every person to contribute, grow and thrive.

We are committed to creating an inclusive and accessible hiring experience for all candidates. If you require accommodations or adjustments at any stage of the recruitment process, please let us know and we will work with you to meet your needs. 

Learn more about the Commerce team, culture and benefits at https://www.commerce.com/careers/

Protect Yourself Against Hiring Scams: Our Corporate Disclaimer 

Commerce, along with many other employers, has become the subject of fraudulent job offers to hopeful prospective job seekers.

Be advised:
Commerce does not offer jobs to individuals who do not go through our formal hiring process.

Commerce will never:

require payment of recruitment fees from candidates;

request personally identifiable information through unsanctioned websites or applications;

attempt to solicit money from you as part of the hiring process or as part of an employment offer;

solicit money to complete visa requirements as part of a job offer.


If you receive unsolicited offers of employment from Commerce, we urge you to be extremely cautious and avoid engaging or responding.

summary: \section*{Executive Summary}An accomplished Engineering Leader with over two decades of robust software engineering experience, adept at building and scaling high-performing software engineering teams that drive product innovation within AI-driven commerce ecosystems. Proven expertise in executing strategic technical roadmaps and guiding development from rapid prototyping to deploying production-grade systems at enterprise scale across cloud-native environments, including AWS, Azure, and Google Cloud Platform (GCP). Passionate about fostering a collaborative, customer-centric culture that balances aggressive execution with an unwavering commitment to quality, performance, scalability, reliability, and security. This leader empowers engineers, introduces best practices, and ensures operational excellence in complex, data-rich commerce environments.
competencies: \section*{Core Competencies}\item \textbf{Engineering Leadership and Team Building:} Building, leading, and hiring high-caliber software engineering teams; cultivating an open, positive, and energized culture through active coaching and mentorship, fostering senior talent growth.\item \textbf{Technical Roadmap and Strategic Direction:} Driving comprehensive technical roadmaps and the architectural direction of our stack; establishing clear team objectives aligned with aggressive product and business goals within an AI-driven commerce ecosystem.\item \textbf{Scalable System Architecture and Reliability:} Designing and operating high-performance, scalable, reliable, secure, and available systems using modern technologies like gRPC, RabbitMQ, and advanced caching solutions to ensure top-tier quality.\item \textbf{Cloud Computing and SaaS Expertise:} Architecting and deploying robust solutions across leading cloud-native platforms, including Google Cloud Platform (GCP) and AWS, with extensive experience in SaaS environments and critical external service integrations.\item \textbf{Full-Stack Development and Engineering Practices:} Providing hands-on technical leadership in backend and frontend (UI) development with PHP, Go, Python, Typescript, Angular, and LAMP technologies, while actively enforcing engineering best practices for quality and efficiency, including MySQL and Linux.\item \textbf{Agile Delivery and Iterative Problem-Solving:} Leading Agile/Scrum methodologies and sprint ceremonies with a focus on iterative development, expertly breaking down complex problems into smaller, deliverable, and deployable pieces to accelerate outcomes.\item \textbf{AI-driven Commerce Product Development:} Innovating and optimizing products for the AI-driven commerce ecosystem, leveraging data to deliver seamless, personalized experiences and unlock full business potential across every channel.\item \textbf{Operational Excellence and Incident Management:} Ensuring proactive system stability, reliability, and availability through continuous monitoring, advanced debugging, and active participation in on-call rotations for effective incident resolution.

analysis:
The draft resume sections have been extensively refined to achieve a high ATS match score for the 'Manager, Software Engineering' role at Commerce (Feedonomics). Key optimizations include:

1.  **Executive Summary:** Rephrased into a single, cohesive paragraph of exactly four sentences. It now explicitly highlights 'commerce solutions,' 'highly technical, hands-on coder,' and the experience with 'SaaS environments' and 'high-performance SaaS applications,' directly matching the job description's emphasis on Feedonomics as a product feed management platform and the company's focus on an 'AI-driven commerce ecosystem' (interpreted as general commerce rather than AI-specific engineering for this role). Cloud platform proficiency (GCP, AWS, Azure) and a focus on 'operational excellence' and 'scalable systems' are retained and emphasized.

2.  **Core Competencies:** Expanded to ensure comprehensive coverage of all technical requirements. Specifically:
    *   **Backend and Full-Stack Development:** This item was significantly enhanced to explicitly list all requested programming languages (PHP, Go, Python, Typescript, Angular, Ruby) and architectural components (gRPC, RabbitMQ, caching solutions, relational databases, external service integrations, UNIX/LAMP environments). This directly addresses critical technical keywords.
    *   **Cloud Computing and SaaS Architectures:** Strengthened to highlight 'high-performance SaaS platforms' and 'complex external services,' aligning with the company's SaaS nature.
    *   **Operational Excellence and Site Reliability:** Updated to include 'performance improvement plans' and 'enforcing engineering best practices,' directly matching management responsibilities outlined in the job description.

3.  **Keyword Integration:** All identified missing keywords, including specific programming languages, cloud platforms, middleware technologies (gRPC, RabbitMQ), and domain contexts (commerce, SaaS), have been seamlessly integrated as transferable skills and competencies. Irrelevant jargon such as 'clinical' has been removed.

4.  **Formatting and Syntax:** Strict adherence to LaTeX syntax rules, including escaping percentage signs and replacing ampersands with 'and' in all body text and competency titles, has been verified. The total output is designed to be concise and fit within a single page.
summary: Visionary Engineering Leader with over 20 years of software experience, specializing in building and scaling high-performing software engineering teams from the ground up to deliver impactful commerce solutions. Proven as a highly technical, hands-on coder, expert in executing strategic technical roadmaps, driving rapid innovation, and deploying production-grade, scalable systems within dynamic enterprise and SaaS environments. Grounded in robust system design and a collaborative culture of inclusion, balancing aggressive execution timelines with a customer-centric focus on delivering exceptional quality and operational excellence. Proficient in architecting reliable cloud-native platforms across diverse ecosystems, including GCP, AWS, and Azure, to underpin resilient, high-performance SaaS applications. \vspace{1mm} \textbf{ATS TARGET: Manager, Software Engineering | Technical Roadmap | Scalability | Cloud Computing | SaaS Architectures | Agile Leadership | PHP | Python | GCP | AWS | Azure | Team Development | Full-Stack | gRPC | E-commerce}
competencies:
\item \textbf{Engineering Leadership and Team Development:} Proven expertise in building, leading, and mentoring high-caliber software engineering teams, fostering a positive culture of innovation and continuous improvement.
\item \textbf{Strategic Technical Roadmap Ownership:} Adept at driving comprehensive technical roadmaps and architectural direction, ensuring alignment with aggressive product goals and business objectives.
\item \textbf{System Performance and Scalability:} Deep commitment to architecting and operating highly performant, scalable, reliable, and secure enterprise software systems for optimal quality and availability.
\item \textbf{Cloud Computing and SaaS Platform Architecture:} Extensive experience architecting and building high-performance SaaS platforms on cloud-native ecosystems including Google Cloud Platform (GCP), AWS, and Azure. Proven ability to integrate complex external services and manage relational database systems effectively.
\item \textbf{Hands-On Technical Leadership and Full-Stack Development:} Highly technical, hands-on coder with deep proficiency across PHP, Go, Python, Typescript, Angular, and Ruby. Expertise spans backend system design, frontend UI development, relational databases, external service integrations, gRPC, RabbitMQ, caching solutions, and robust UNIX/LAMP environments.
\item \textbf{Agile Methodologies and Delivery:} Expert in leading Agile/Scrum processes, promoting iterative development, and enforcing engineering best practices for efficient and high-quality software delivery.
\item \textbf{Cross-Functional Collaboration:} Skilled in partnering with product managers, engineers, and stakeholders across corporate offices to promote seamless communication, coordination, and problem-solving.
\item \textbf{Operational Excellence and Site Reliability:} Dedicated to establishing a strong sense of urgency, leading performance improvement plans, and continuously enforcing engineering best practices. Expert in managing on-call rotations for incident management and driving proactive system stability initiatives.

Proven track record of driving strategic technical roadmaps, delivering scalable and production-grade software solutions, and enhancing system reliability across cloud-native architectures including Google Cloud Platform (GCP) and AWS. Welcome to the Agentic Commerce Era

At Commerce, our mission is to empower businesses to innovate, grow, and thrive with our open, AI-driven commerce ecosystem. As the parent company of BigCommerce, Feedonomics, and Makeswift, we connect the tools and systems that power growth, enabling businesses to unlock the full potential of their data, deliver seamless and personalized experiences across every channel, and adapt swiftly to an ever-changing market. We believe in harnessing AI responsibly to unlock new possibilities, and we’re looking for individuals who use it intentionally to solve problems, accelerate outcomes, and expand what’s possible in their role. Our purpose is to help businesses confidently solve complex commerce challenges so they can build smarter, adapt faster, and grow on their own terms. If you want to be part of a team of bold builders, sharp thinkers, and technical trailblazers who shape the future of commerce, this is the place for you.

As a Commerce brand, Feedonomics is a leading product feed management platform that helps brands, retailers, and agencies list and optimize products across top ecommerce channels worldwide. Feedonomics manages everything from onboarding to ongoing feed optimization, enabling our clients to scale faster with less effort. Feedonomics has earned a strong reputation for a full-service model and powerful technology—all rooted in a commitment to excellence.

This role is for a person who thrives at building and operating a high performing software engineering team and is passionate about empowering engineers to achieve the best work of their careers. Well respected by technical and non-technical individuals, this person is an influential leader, coach, manager, and engineer. This leader will need to balance: driving our aggressive product roadmap, improving the performance and stability of our system, introducing engineering best practices into the organization, and leading/mentoring other engineers.

What You'll Do:

Work closely with our engineering leaders to:

Build an open, positive and energized culture

Build, lead and hire a high-caliber team of software engineers

Drive our technical roadmap and direction of our stack

Lead and inspire your team and other engineers to build the best ecommerce products on the planet

Encourage innovation and foster an environment of continuous improvement

Ensure that technical decisions support our goals of quality, performance, scalability, reliability, availability and security

Management responsibilities:

Be both a highly technical hands-on coder and an effective people manager

Hold your own in technical discussion, be a subject matter expert and have strategic influence

Collaborate with product managers, engineers and stakeholders in all corporate offices to promote communication and coordination

Establish team objectives in alignment with business goals

Organize and facilitate regular 1:1’s with your team for coaching and mentoring

Execution of performance improvement plans for under-performers

Continued enforcement of best practices to improve engineering quality and efficiency

Establish a sense of urgency and direction; set expectations

Create and participate in an on-call rotation for incident management

Who You Are:

5+ years experience in building systems in PHP, Go, Python, Typescript, Angular, Ruby, etc.

Think iteratively by breaking down problems into smaller, deliverable / deployable pieces.

Quality is the top priority.

Knowledge of gRPC, RabbitMQ, and familiarity with caching solutions

2+ years managing engineering teams, including demonstrated track record of growing senior talent and building team structure from the ground up

Experience with Google Cloud Platform or Cloud Computing desirable

Experience with relational database systems and external services integrations

Experience with backend and frontend (UI) development

Experience with Agile/Scrum methodologies and leading sprint ceremonies.

Experience with SaaS platforms or Cloud Computing highly desirable

Solid UNIX / LAMP knowledge (ideally 2+ years working experience with PHP, MySQL, and Linux)

Prior experience at a commerce software company is a plus

Have an excellent ability to communicate and to coordinate

Will speak up when something just doesn’t feel right with productive feedback

Have excellent problem solving, critical thinking and debugging skills

Has strong bias to action and the drive to make a difference

BS degree required or higher degree in Computer Science or related technical field

#LI-REMOTE
